alignments.preprint_claim_text: Functional connectivity was significantly decreased in MDD in a neural network connecting frontal, temporal and occipital areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Functional connectivity was significantly decreased in MDD in a neural network connecting frontal, temporal and occipital areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text is effectively identical between preprint and publication with no change in scope or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a significant association between network connectivity and trait neuroticism. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a significant association between network connectivity and trait neuroticism. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording is the same but the publication reports a higher certainty, indicating increased confidence in the finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study relates a network-based phenotype for altered pain processing and antinociceptive control to MDD. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Altered pain processing and antinociceptive control are related to a network-based phenotype for MDD. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same relationship with slightly rephrased wording and reports lower certainty, so the claim is clarified in wording and tempered in confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future studies are encouraged on the shared intermediate neural psychological risk architecture of MDD and chronic pain. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study encourages future research on the shared intermediate neural psychological risk architecture of MDD and chronic pain. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the encouragement for future research with equivalent phrasing and the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to decode the locus of auditory attention within 1 to 2 seconds using a convolutional neural network-based approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to decode the locus of auditory attention within 1-2 seconds using a convolutional neural network-based approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are the same; only minor formatting (1 to 2 vs 1-2 seconds) differs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median accuracy of decoding the locus of attention is around 81%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The median accuracy of decoding the locus of attention is around 81%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical numeric result and wording between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method does not require knowledge of the speech envelopes to extract the locus of auditory attention. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method does not require knowledge of the speech envelopes to classify auditory attention. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Meaning is preserved though the publication rephrases 'extract the locus' as 'classify auditory attention'; core claim and confidence unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results are promising for neuro-steered noise suppression in hearing aids. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results are promising for neuro-steered noise suppression in hearing aids. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion and expressed certainty between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNN architecture, specifically convolutional filter size and max-pooling, influences the extent that sequence motif representations are learned by first layer filters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNN architecture, specifically convolutional filter size and max-pooling, influences the extent that sequence motif representations are learned by first layer filters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is stated identically in preprint and publication with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNNs designed to foster hierarchical representation learning of sequence motifs tend to learn distributed representations, i.e. partial motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNNs designed to foster hierarchical representation learning of sequence motifs tend to learn distributed representations, i.e. partial motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is effectively identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNNs that limit the ability to hierarchically build sequence motif representations tend to learn more interpretable localist representations, i.e. whole motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNNs that limit the ability to hierarchically build sequence motif representations in deeper layers tend to learn more interpretable localist representations, i.e. whole motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the qualifier 'in deeper layers', narrowing where the limitation applies; core finding and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representation learning principle established from synthetic sequences generalizes to in vivo sequences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation learning principle established from synthetic sequences generalizes to in vivo sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Textual content is the same; classification shifted from 'conclusion' to 'result' but the claim content, scope, and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNN models outperformed all the other baseline models for V1 cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNN models outperformed all the other baseline models for V1 cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and certainty in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two key factors that made CNNs outperform other models are thresholding nonlinearity and convolution. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two key factors that made CNNs outperform other models are thresholding nonlinearity and convolution. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No change in wording, scope, or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The higher layers of the pre-trained deep CNN, which encode more complex patterns, outperformed the lower layers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The higher layers of the pre-trained deep CNN, which encode more complex patterns, outperformed the lower layers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results are consistent with earlier work on the complexity of V1 neural code. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results are consistent with earlier work on the complexity of V1 neural code. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Statement is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diffusion model accounts for much more variance in functional connectivity than the shortest path routing model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diffusion model accounts for much more variance in functional connectivity than the shortest path routing model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and expressed certainty remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diffusion model is better suited to describing the structure-function relationship in the human brain at the macroscale. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diffusion model is better suited to describing the structure–function relationship in the human brain at the macroscale. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion but reports reduced certainty (high -> medium), indicating tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hybrid measures that use concepts from both the shortest path routing model and the diffusion model have better predicted functional connectivity from structural connectivity than shortest path length alone. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hybrid measures that use concepts from both the shortest path routing model and the diffusion model have better predicted functional connectivity from structural connectivity than the shortest path length alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is the same but the publication reports higher certainty for this result, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Antibiotic resistance in E. coli can be accurately predicted from whole genome sequences without a priori knowledge of mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Antibiotic resistance in E. coli can be accurately predicted from whole genome sequences without a priori knowledge of mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The two statements are effectively identical in wording and scope, asserting the same result with the same confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradient boosted decision trees consistently outperformed alternative models with an average F1 score of 0.88 on held-out data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient boosted decision trees consistently outperformed alternative models with an average accuracy of 0.91 on held-out data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state that gradient boosted trees outperformed alternatives, but the publication changes the reported metric and gives a higher numeric performance, so the claim is revised and strengthened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An average F1 score of 0.73 could be obtained using population structure information alone. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An average accuracy score of 0.79 could be obtained using population structure information alone. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim (population structure alone yields predictive signal) is retained but the reported metric and value change in the publication, reflecting a revised (and numerically higher) result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Single nucleotide variation data were less useful and failed to improve prediction for ten out of 11 antibiotics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Single nucleotide variation data were less useful and significantly improved prediction only for two antibiotics, including ciprofloxacin. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert SNV data were generally less useful but the publication reports improvement for two antibiotics rather than only one, changing the numeric outcome and softening the absolute failure statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the potential application of machine learning methods as a diagnostic tool in healthcare settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of machine learning approaches into diagnostic tools in the clinic is now feasible. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication advances the preprint's notion of potential application to a stronger feasibility claim; the conclusion is therefore revised and presented with greater practical assertiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach reveals variable 8-oxo-dG distribution across the genome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method reveals uneven genomic 8-oxo-dG distribution and a dissimilar context pattern to C>A mutations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the observation of uneven/variable genomic distribution directly, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The context pattern of 8-oxo-dG is dissimilar to C>A mutations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method reveals uneven genomic 8-oxo-dG distribution and a dissimilar context pattern to C>A mutations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly includes the same observation about context pattern dissimilarity, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study provides insights into the functional interplay between 5-mC and 8-oxo-dG at single molecule resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dual measurement of 5-mC and 8-oxo-dG at single-molecule resolution uncovers new insights into their interplay. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same dual-measurement insight at single-molecule resolution, preserving the claim content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work contributes to advancing the field of epigenetics and highlights nanopore sequencing as a powerful tool for studying DNA modifications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed strategy using synthetic oligos enables the generation of long, 8-oxo-dG context-variable DNA molecules for deep learning and nanopore sequencing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific methodological detail (synthetic oligos enabling nanopore sequencing) that clarifies and grounds the preprint's broader contribution about advancing the field and highlighting nanopore sequencing.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach provides a general framework for detecting other rare DNA modifications using synthetic DNA and nanopore sequencing. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This publication-level claim about generalizing the approach to other rare modifications is not present in the preprint and thus appears as a new/added contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel sequential approaches through recurrent neural networks can improve the accuracy of T-cell epitope immunogenicity predictions in mice. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel sequential approaches through recurrent neural networks can improve the accuracy of T-cell epitope binding affinity predictions in mice. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the target from 'immunogenicity' to 'binding affinity', a substantive change that narrows the biological claim while preserving the overall methodological conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed software can estimate H-2 peptide ligands, with an AUC of 0.95, directly from tumor samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed software can estimate H-2 peptide ligands, with an AUC comparable or superior to state-of-the-art methods, directly from tumor samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific numeric AUC as a comparative performance statement against state-of-the-art methods, clarifying performance context rather than contradicting the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We report the putative neoantigens identified using the B16 melanoma model as a proof-of-concept for the system’s predictive capabilities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We report the putative neoantigens identified using the B16 melanoma model as a proof-of-concept for the system’s predictive capabilities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The proof-of-concept report is verbatim between versions and unchanged in scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simplified variant selection process can reduce operational requirements for neoantigen discovery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simplified variant selection process can reduce operational requirements for neoantigen discovery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This operational/conceptual conclusion is preserved unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of existing computational cell type proportion estimation methods is significantly affected by the mismatch between training data and real-world data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of existing computational cell type proportion estimation methods is significantly affected by the mismatch between training data and real-world data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint result verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DAISM-DNN XMBD1 pipeline utilizes a novel Data Augmentation method with an In Silico Mixing strategy to improve training data quality. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DAISM-DNN pipeline, which utilizes a novel data augmentation method, effectively addresses the issues caused by training data mismatch. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the methodological contribution in terms of addressing the training-data mismatch; content is the same but presented with clarified emphasis on effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation results demonstrate that the DAISM-DNN pipeline consistently and substantially outperforms other existing methods for all evaluated cell types on real-world datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluation results indicate that the DAISM-DNN pipeline consistently and substantially outperforms other existing methods for all evaluated cell types in real-world datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The evaluation result is restated with equivalent strength and scope in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An unsupervised deep neural network model that combines matrix factorization and conditional variational autoencoders is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a deep neural network model called restricted latent variational autoencoder (resVAE) that integrates matrix factorization and variational autoencoders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication names and formalizes the proposed model but retains the same core contribution of combining matrix factorization and VAEs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model utilizes weights as matrix factorizations to obtain gene sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resVAE model uses weights as factorized matrices to obtain gene sets and facilitates plausible identification of cell types through class-specific inputs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same technical result about weight-based factorization to derive gene sets; additional functions are described but this claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model facilitates a plausible identification of cell types through class-specific inputs to the latent variable space. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resVAE model uses weights as factorized matrices to obtain gene sets and facilitates plausible identification of cell types through class-specific inputs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the cell-type identification claim with the matrix factorization description and presents it with higher certainty, clarifying and strengthening the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The artificial neural network model integrates functional gene set inference, experimental batch effect correction, and static gene identification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model seamlessly integrates functional gene set inference, experimental covariate effect isolation, and static gene identification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the integrated functionalities of the model with equivalent meaning, using slightly different wording for covariate/batch effect handling.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the model is conceptually proven for three single-cell RNA-Seq datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We conceptually demonstrate the application of the resVAE model for four single-cell RNA sequencing datasets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the reported number of demonstration datasets from three to four and reduces certainty from high to medium, altering both scope and expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hindered diffusion model is directly supported by single-molecule measurements in a developing embryo. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hindered diffusion model is directly supported by measurements and simulations of the movement and binding of signaling molecules in live developing zebrafish embryos. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion while adding measurements/simulations and specifying zebrafish embryos, which clarifies and contextualizes the original claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nodal and Lefty exhibit different global diffusivities due to differential hindrance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diffusion coefficients of signaling molecules are high in extracellular cavities but reduced at cell-cell interfaces. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized molecule-specific differences (Nodal vs Lefty due to hindrance), while the publication emphasizes spatial differences in diffusion; this shifts the focus away from the original molecule-comparison claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The geometry of the extracellular space significantly influences the accumulation of signaling molecules in cavities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Molecules accumulate in extracellular cavities due to the geometry of the extracellular space, despite reduced mobility at cell-cell interfaces. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same idea that geometry drives accumulation but adds the caveat about reduced mobility at interfaces, clarifying the mechanism and context of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nodal molecules have a larger fraction in the bound state and longer binding times compared to Lefty molecules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nodal has a larger bound fraction than Lefty and exhibits a binding time of tens of seconds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same molecule-specific binding differences and adds a quantitative binding-time estimate, which clarifies but does not change the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our novel method identified and extracted DTIs from 2.1 million studies not previously included in public DTI databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our novel method identified 0.6 million articles which are not previously included in public DTI databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim structure but reports a substantially smaller number, which revises and tempers the original numerical result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using 10-fold cross-validation, we obtained ~99% accuracy for identifying studies containing drug-target pairs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using 10-fold cross-validation, we obtained ~99% accuracy for identifying articles containing quantitative drug-target profiles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same performance number while clarifying the target as quantitative drug-target profiles, keeping the claim intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy for the prediction of assay format is ~90%, which leaves room for improvement in future studies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The F1 micro for the prediction of assay format is 88%, which leaves room for improvement in future studies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the metric and gives a specific performance number (F1 micro, 88%), clarifying the preprint's approximate 90% statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BERT model in this study is robust and the proposed pipeline can be used to identify new and previously overlooked studies containing DTIs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The BERT model in this study is robust and the proposed pipeline can be used to identify previously overlooked articles containing quantitative DTIs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion and refines wording to emphasize quantitative DTIs, clarifying but not changing the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method provides a significant advancement in machine-assisted DTI extraction and curation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method provides a significant advancement in machine-assisted DTI extraction and curation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cortical activity propagations robustly ascend and descend the cortical hierarchy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cortical activity propagations systematically ascend and descend a cortical hierarchy in all individuals in the developmental cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the phenomenon is systematic and observed in all individuals of the cohort, clarifying and strengthening the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Top-down propagations become more prevalent with cognitive control demands. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Top-down, descending hierarchical propagations become more prevalent with greater demands for cognitive control in youth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the association with cognitive control demands but clarifies the population (youth) and increases certainty, amplifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Top-down propagations become more prevalent with development in youth. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Top-down propagations may serve as a potential mechanism of neurocognitive maturation in youth. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the developmental role of top-down propagations more cautiously as a possible mechanism (lower certainty) rather than asserting prevalence increases, representing a revision with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PhenoVision achieves high accuracy for detecting flower presence at 98.5% and fruit presence at 95%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PhenoVision achieves high accuracy on held-out test images for flower (98.5%) and fruit presence (95%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same evaluation results on held-out tests, matching the preprint's performance claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of iNaturalist data significantly increases phenology data coverage, especially in previously limited regions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effort vastly increases phenology data coverage, including regions of the globe where data have been limited before. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the contribution of expanding coverage, explicitly noting previously limited regions, which matches the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Post-calibration tuning and validation are essential for producing research-ready phenology data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PhenoVision demonstrates a high level of agreement with an expert annotator (98.6% for flowers and 90.4% for fruits). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete validation results (agreement with expert annotator) that relate to the preprint's emphasis on calibration/validation, clarifying and supporting the methodological claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A standardized set of quality metrics and metadata has been developed to enhance the usability of phenology data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A standardized set of quality metrics and metadata has been developed to ensure results can be used effectively by the community. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same contribution about standardized metrics and metadata with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Humans focused on shape and overlooked many non-shape features, even when non-shape features were more diagnostic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Humans focused on shape and overlooked many non-shape features, even when non-shape features were more diagnostic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this experimental result verbatim, showing no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Humans learned based on only one out of multiple predictive features and failed to learn when global features, such as shape, were absent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Humans learned based on only one out of multiple predictive features and failed to learn when global features, such as shape, were absent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same result without substantive modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNNs preferred to categorise objects based on non-shape features and increased reliance on these non-shape features as they became more predictive. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNNs preferred to categorise objects based on non-shape features and increased reliance on these non-shape features as they became more predictive. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This experimental result is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shape-bias has a different source in humans and CNNs, with learning in CNNs driven by the statistical properties of the environment, while humans are constrained by their previous biases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shape-bias has a different source in humans and CNNs, with learning in CNNs driven by the statistical properties of the environment, while humans are constrained by their previous biases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this interpretive conclusion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models that incorporate cognitive constraints may help develop machine learning models that are more robust to changes in incidental features of objects. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This suggested contribution about incorporating cognitive constraints does not appear in the publication, so the claim was removed or omitted in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A convolutional neural network (CNN) was developed to automatically detect saccades at human-level performance accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a convolutional neural network to automatically detect saccades at human-level accuracy and with minimal training examples.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core result but adds the detail about requiring minimal training examples, which clarifies rather than revises the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm surpasses state of the art according to common performance metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our algorithm surpasses state of the art according to common performance metrics.
alignments.rationale: Text and certainty are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed algorithm will facilitate studies of neurophysiological processes underlying saccade generation and visual processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our algorithm could facilitate studies of neurophysiological processes underlying saccade generation and visual processing.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the predicted impact from 'will' to 'could', clarifying and reducing expressed confidence while keeping the same scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We show that our algorithm performs better than other available algorithms, by comparing performance on a wide range of data sets.
alignments.rationale: This explicit comparative claim is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We offer an open-source implementation of the algorithm as well as a web service.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a concrete implementation/service contribution not mentioned in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neuronal activity during rest periods in honey bees exhibits stereotypic patterns comparable to sleep signatures observed in vertebrates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neuronal activity during rest periods in honey bees exhibits stereotypic patterns comparable to sleep signatures observed in vertebrates.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated verbatim with unchanged certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Out-of-sample classification accuracy reached 93% when distinguishing between motion and rest states in honey bees.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Out-of-sample classification accuracy reached 93% when distinguishing between motion and rest states in honey bees.
alignments.rationale: Identical performance claim and confidence in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The glomerular connectivity in the antennal lobes was significantly increased in the rest-state patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The glomerular connectivity was found to be significantly increased in the rest-state patterns of honey bees.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GABAergic system plays a central role in sleep regulation in honey bees, similar to its role in higher species including humans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GABAergic system plays a central role in sleep regulation in honey bees, similar to its role in higher species including humans.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried over unchanged in content and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that sleep-related network modulation mechanisms may be conserved throughout evolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings support the theoretical view that sleep-related network modulation mechanisms are conserved throughout evolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the evolutionary implication slightly more assertively ('support the theoretical view' vs 'suggest ... may be'), a modest clarification without scope change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sense of agency (SoA) can be decoded from the same brain structures that implement inverse dynamics computations for planning movement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sense of agency (SoA) can be decoded from the same brain structures that implement inverse dynamics computations for planning movement.
alignments.rationale: The core claim appears verbatim but the publication assigns higher certainty (medium -> high), so confidence is amplified while semantic content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The same voxels best explained by modeled inverse dynamics representations also predicted SoA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Manipulating SoA by usurping control of participants' muscles via electrical stimulation revealed that the same voxels best explained by modeled inverse dynamics representations also predicted SoA.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds experimental manipulation detail (clarifying evidence) but reports this with slightly lower certainty than the preprint, hence tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model-brain correspondences and robust SoA decoding could both be achieved within single subjects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-brain correspondences and robust SoA decoding can be achieved within single subjects, allowing for the study of relationships between motor representations and awareness at the individual level.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands on the implication for individual-level study but retains the original claim that these correspondences can be achieved within single subjects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding which motor control representations percolate into awareness is increasingly important as neural interface technologies advance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-brain correspondences and robust SoA decoding can be achieved within single subjects, allowing for the study of relationships between motor representations and awareness at the individual level.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general motivation about importance of understanding motor representations is not explicitly restated, but the publication emphasizes the individual-level study enabled by the results, which is the closest match.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Detailed cortical encodings of sensorimotor states are idiosyncratic to each subject.
alignments.rationale: This specific result about idiosyncratic cortical encodings does not appear in the preprint and therefore is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EfficientNet-B3 demonstrated superior performance in classifying images of sorghum infected with charcoal rot, achieving an accuracy of 86.97%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EfficientNet‐B3 demonstrated superior performance in classifying images of sorghum infected with charcoal rot, achieving an accuracy of 86.97%.
alignments.rationale: Identical numerical result and wording retained in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fully convolutional network (FCN) proved to be the most effective segmentation model, exhibiting a validation accuracy of 97.76%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fully convolutional network (FCN) proved to be the most effective segmentation model, exhibiting a validation accuracy of 97.76%.
alignments.rationale: The segmentation result is repeated verbatim with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Larger image patch sizes generally improve model performance and reduce processing time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Larger image patch sizes generally improve model performance and reduce processing time.
alignments.rationale: The methodological conclusion is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models can assist in the prompt identification and management of charcoal rot in sorghum, potentially reducing yield losses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models developed can assist breeders and growers of sorghum in the detection and management of charcoal rot.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the target audience (breeders/growers) and states the assistance more directly with higher certainty, clarifying and amplifying the applied claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models advance the domain of automated plant phenotyping and may serve as a base for drone-based or other automated field phenotyping efforts.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the preprint's forward-looking claim about drone-based/field phenotyping; that suggested application is not carried over.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The models can be accessed through a web‐based application for users to analyze their own images.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a concrete access mechanism (web application) that was not present in the preprint, representing an added contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cognitive performance can be predicted from an individual’s functional brain connectivity with modest accuracy using machine learning approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cognitive performance can be predicted from an individual's functional brain connectivity with modest accuracy using machine learning approaches.
alignments.rationale: The primary result is restated with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature weight reliability is generally poor for all predictive models of cognitive performance, with an ICC of less than 0.3.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature weight reliability is generally poor for all predictive models of cognitive performance, with an ICC of less than 0.3.
alignments.rationale: The reliability result is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Larger sample sizes and certain methodological adjustments marginally improve feature weight reliability, but it remains below 0.4.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Larger sample sizes and certain methodological adjustments marginally improve feature weight reliability, but it remains below 0.4.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward this nuanced quantitative result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a tradeoff between feature weight reliability and prediction accuracy, with univariate statistics being marginally more reliable than feature weights from predictive models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a tradeoff between feature weight reliability and prediction accuracy, with univariate statistics being marginally more reliable than feature weights from predictive models.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the tradeoff is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reliability estimates of feature weights should be calculated out-of-sample to avoid inflated estimates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reliability should be estimated out-of-sample to avoid inflated estimates of feature weight reliability.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation is restated with effectively the same content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine-learning model demonstrated a mean sensitivity of 0.90, specificity of 0.92, and ROC-AUC of 0.93 for predicting KRT81+ vs. KRT81- tumor subtypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine-learning model demonstrated a mean sensitivity of 0.90, specificity of 0.92, and ROC-AUC of 0.93 for predicting KRT81+ vs. KRT81- tumor subtypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication texts are identical in metrics and claim; no substantive change is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with a KRT81+ subtype experienced significantly diminished median overall survival compared to KRT81- patients (7.0 vs. 22.6 months, HR 1.44, log-rank-test P=<0.001). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with a KRT81+ subtype experienced significantly diminished median overall survival compared to KRT81- patients (7.0 vs. 22.6 months, HR 4.03, log-rank-test P = <0.001). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The direction and significance of the survival difference are preserved but the hazard ratio changed substantially in the publication, representing a numerical revision and a stronger reported effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KRT81+ patients had a significantly improved response to gemcitabine-based chemotherapy compared to KRT81- patients, who responded better to FOLFIRINOX. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This treatment-response claim appears in the preprint but is absent from the publication, indicating the claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine-learning based analysis of radiomic features enables the prediction of subtypes of PDAC, which are highly relevant for overall patient survival and response to chemotherapy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine-learning based analysis of radiomic features enables the prediction of subtypes of PDAC, which are highly relevant for disease-free and overall patient survival and response to chemotherapy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion but explicitly adds 'disease-free' survival, broadening the outcome relevance while keeping the core claim intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SciPipe is a workflow programming library implemented in the programming language Go, designed for managing complex and dynamic pipelines in bioinformatics and cheminformatics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SciPipe is a workflow programming library implemented in the programming language Go, for managing complex and dynamic pipelines in bioinformatics, cheminformatics, and other fields. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the stated application domains ('and other fields') while keeping the core contribution the same, a clarification/broadening of scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SciPipe supports workflow constructs common in machine learning, such as extensive branching, parameter sweeps, and dynamic scheduling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SciPipe helps in particular with workflow constructs common in machine learning, such as extensive branching, parameter sweeps, and dynamic scheduling and parametrization of downstream tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds detail about parametrization of downstream tasks but preserves the original claim about ML-oriented workflow constructs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SciPipe enables agile development of workflows based on a library of self-contained, reusable components. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SciPipe provides a solution for agile development of complex and dynamic pipelines, especially in machine learning, through a flexible application programming interface suitable for scientists used to programming or scripting. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the agile development claim with emphasis on API flexibility and target users but conveys the same conclusion about enabling agile workflow development.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SciPipe provides a data-centric audit logging feature that saves a full audit trace for every output file of a workflow. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SciPipe supports running subsets of workflows for improved iterative development and provides a data-centric audit logging feature that saves a full audit trace for every output file of a workflow. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the audit logging claim and additionally describes running workflow subsets, so the original claim is clarified and placed within a broader feature context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generalized Matrix Learning Vector Quantization (GMLVQ) provides comprehensive visualization capabilities not present in other classifiers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generalized Matrix Learning Vector Quantization (GMLVQ) provides comprehensive visualization capabilities not present in other classifiers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim is verbatim between preprint and publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GMLVQ is well-suited for imbalanced classification problems which are frequent in life sciences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GMLVQ is well-suited for imbalanced classification problems which are frequent in life sciences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is unchanged in wording and implication between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feasibility of GMLVQ is demonstrated on a dataset of Early Folding Residues (EFR) with an area under the receiver operating characteristic of 76.6%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feasibility of GMLVQ is demonstrated on a dataset of Early Folding Residues (EFR) with an area under the receiver operating characteristic of 76.6%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result and reported metric are identical in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application on EFR prediction demonstrates how an easy interpretation of classification models can promote the comprehension of biological mechanisms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application on EFR prediction demonstrates how an easy interpretation of classification models can promote the comprehension of biological mechanisms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretability conclusion is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results shed light on the special features of EFR which are embedded in ordered secondary structure elements and participate in networks of hydrophobic residues. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results shed light on the special features of EFR which are embedded in ordered secondary structure elements and participate in networks of hydrophobic residues. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The descriptive result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction algorithm achieves AUROC of 0.872 for AKI onset detection, superior to the SOFA score AUROC of 0.815. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm achieves AUROC of 0.872 for AKI detection at time of onset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUROC value for detection at onset but omits the direct SOFA comparison present in the preprint, making this a clarification with loss of the comparative detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At 72 hours before onset, the algorithm achieves AUROC of 0.728, compared to the SOFA score AUROC of 0.720. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm demonstrated high AUROC for detecting and predicting NHS-defined AKI at all tested time points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a more general claim about high AUROC at all time points without reporting the specific 72-hour numeric comparison, so the specific preprint result is generalized/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine-learning-based AKI prediction tool may offer important prognostic capabilities for determining which patients are likely to suffer AKI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm may offer important prognostic capabilities for determining which patients are likely to suffer AKI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about potential prognostic utility is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MVPAlab is a MATLAB-based toolbox designed for multidimensional electroencephalography and magnetoencephalography data analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MVPAlab is a MATLAB-based toolbox designed for multidimensional electroencephalography and magnetoencephalography data analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant clusters (p<0.01) were found for the proposed decoding analyses, demonstrating the software's capability for discriminating between different experimental conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant clusters (p<0.01) were found for the proposed decoding analyses, demonstrating the software's capability for discriminating between different experimental conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported statistical result and interpretation are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MVPAlab includes an easy-to-use graphic user interface, making it convenient for users with little or no coding experience. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MVPAlab includes an easy-to-use graphic user interface, making it convenient for users with few or no previous coding experience. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the certainty level and slightly tweaks phrasing about target users, clarifying and increasing confidence in the GUI usability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The toolbox implements several high and low-level routines, allowing experienced users to design their own projects flexibly. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MVPAlab implements several high and low-level routines that allow experienced users to design their own projects in a highly flexible manner. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the functionality claim but increases the stated certainty and emphasizes flexibility, representing a clarified and amplified claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cryo-electron tomography (cryo-ET) allows visualization of the cellular context at the macromolecular level. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cryo-electron tomography (cryo-ET) allows visualization of the cellular context at the macromolecular level. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-certainty conclusion verbatim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current simulators in cryo-ET cannot generate many of the low order features present in cellular tomograms. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current simulators in cryo-ET cannot generate many of the low order features present in cellular tomograms. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation claim is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed geometric and organization models can simulate low order cellular structures imaged by cryo-ET. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose several geometric and organization models to simulate low order cellular structures imaged by cryo-ET. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution (proposal of geometric/organization models) with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implemented models generate a high diversity of geometries and organizations to simulate representative and generalized datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These models generate a high diversity of geometries and organizations to simulate representative and generalized datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about model-generated diversity is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A realistic synthetic dataset can be readily used to train generalizable deep learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A realistic synthetic dataset can be readily used to train generalizable deep learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about using realistic synthetic data for training deep learning models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ensemble classifier achieves a classification accuracy of 94% when categorizing experimental results into eight different classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifiers have different strengths that can be combined to provide an ensemble classifier achieving a classification accuracy comparable to that obtained by a large consortium initiative. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the explicit 94% figure and reframes the result as comparable to a consortium benchmark rather than reporting the exact accuracy for eight classes, weakening and narrowing the original numeric claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different convolutional deep-learning network architectures have varying strengths that can be combined to improve classification performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifiers have different strengths that can be combined to provide an ensemble classifier achieving a classification accuracy comparable to that obtained by a large consortium initiative. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same idea about complementary strengths but clarifies it by showing an ensemble result and states it with higher certainty (comparative benchmark), amplifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification results obtained can provide detailed information for automatically identifying crystal formation in drug discovery. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach provides detailed information that can be used with routine crystallography experiments to automatically identify crystal formation for drug discovery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the application context (with routine crystallography experiments) but preserves the contribution and certainty level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifiers can be implemented without the need for extensive computational resources. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state or support the claimed low computational-resource requirement and instead adds different concluding remarks, so this implementation-efficiency claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CycGANRegNet outperforms multiple state-of-the-art deep learning based and conventional pathology image registration methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CGNReg demonstrates promising performance compared to multiple state-of-the-art systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's stronger 'outperforms' wording is softened in the publication to 'demonstrates promising performance compared to', indicating a revision and tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CycGANRegNet can produce promising registration results with serial WSIs in different stains. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CGNReg can produce promising registration results with serial whole-slide images in different stains. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about producing promising registration results across stains; wording and certainty are effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method enables integrative 3D tissue-based biomedical investigations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method enables integrative 3D tissue-based biomedical investigations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The contribution enabling integrative 3D investigations is preserved with similar phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep-learning methods have significantly improved the prediction accuracy of 3-dimensional protein structures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep-learning methods such as RoseTTAFold and AlphaFold significantly improve the prediction accuracy of 3D protein structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both documents make the same high-certainty claim that deep-learning methods substantially improve 3D structure prediction; the publication gives examples but does not change the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RoseTTAFold was used to predict the coevolution of about 95% of mitochondrial protein pairs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RoseTTAFold was able to predict the coevolution of about 95% of mitochondrial protein pairs with high computational speed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 95% coevolution prediction claim and adds a clarification about computational speed, so the claim is clarified but not contradicted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 produced high-precision contact probability predictions that were consistent with those from RoseTTAFold. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structural models produced by AlphaFold for top-ranked protein pairs were consistent with known protein-protein interactions and experimental structural complexes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from contact-probability agreement with RoseTTAFold to consistency of AlphaFold structural models with known PPIs and complexes for top-ranked pairs, narrowing and softening the original comparative prediction claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified novel protein-protein interactions for several proteins without experimentally characterized interaction partners. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel protein-protein interactions were identified for several proteins without experimentally characterized interaction partners, suggesting new insights into their molecular functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the novel-interaction finding but adds an interpretation about insights into molecular functions, a clarification rather than a contradiction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Good accuracy in inference can be achieved with a relatively small number of simulations using machine learning approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Good accuracy in inference can be achieved with a relatively small number of simulations using machine learning approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same high-certainty result about inference accuracy with few simulations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning approaches are orders of magnitude faster than classical simulation-based methods that rely on sampling the parameter space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning approaches are orders of magnitude faster than classical simulation-based methods that rely on sampling the parameter space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The speed-up claim is retained verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No algorithm offers a one-size-fits-all solution when attempting to infer model parameters from observations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No algorithm offers a one-size-fits-all solution when attempting to infer model parameters from observations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same balanced conclusion about algorithm limitations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stochastic nature of the considered real-world phenomena poses an additional challenge that can become insurmountable for some approaches. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stochastic nature of the considered real-world phenomena poses an additional challenge that can become insurmountable for some approaches. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding stochasticity is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning approaches that create direct inference machines are promising for real-world applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning approaches that create direct inference machines are promising for real-world applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the optimistic conclusion but lowers the expressed certainty (high -> medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FloraTraiter successfully parsed over 95% of treatment content for traits with less than 1% error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FloraTraiter successfully parsed over 95% of treatment content for traits with less than 1% error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact quantitative result and error claim, so there is no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data for more than 700 taxa were reported using the FloraTraiter approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data for more than 700 taxa were reported using the FloraTraiter approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numeric result and claim verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FloraTraiter is a novel approach that utilizes rule-based natural language processing to extract computable trait data from biodiversity literature. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FloraTraiter is a novel approach that utilizes rule-based natural language processing to extract computable trait data from biodiversity literature. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Largely automated tools like FloraTraiter will be an important addition to the toolkit for assembling trait data at scale. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The strategies and applications identified in this study aim to facilitate the production of large open-source trait data sets for community reuse. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the implication toward facilitating large open-source datasets and community reuse rather than simply being an 'addition to the toolkit,' broadening emphasis and clarifying intended outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed technology for identifying and characterizing ignorance statements in biomedical literature could significantly impact various aspects of science, including PhD training and funding prioritization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed novel idea for biomedical natural language processing is to identify and characterize questions stated in the biomedical literature. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the core idea (identifying questions) but drops the strong claim about broad impacts on training and funding, narrowing and tempering the original impact claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel ignorance taxonomy has been developed that identifies specific goals for future scientific knowledge. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel ignorance taxonomy has been created that identifies specific goals for future scientific knowledge. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same contribution (creation of a taxonomy identifying goals), with no meaningful change in claim or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifiers trained on the gold standard ignorance corpus achieved over 0.80 F1 scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classifiers trained on the ignorance corpus achieved over 0.80 F1 scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance metric and claim are restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gold standard ignorance corpus consists of 60 full-text documents from the prenatal nutrition literature with over 10,000 annotations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A gold standard ignorance corpus of 60 full-text documents with over 10,000 annotations has been developed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same corpus size and annotation count, maintaining the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classifier-based machine learning models trained on curated sequence and structural data can effectively predict substrate specificity for glycosyltransferases (GTs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study develops machine learning models that achieve good predictive accuracies for substrate specificity of GT-B glycosyltransferases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report that ML models predict substrate specificity with good accuracy; the publication reframes but preserves the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the machine learning models indicates that both sequence and structural features are important for understanding substrate specificity in GT-Bs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Substrate specificity of enzymes involves an indirect relationship with sequence, influenced by structural aspects that affect substrate accessibility and active site dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands on the mechanistic relationship between sequence and structure, clarifying and broadening the original conclusion about the importance of both feature types.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is potential for further improvement in the training of machine learning models to establish interpretable relationships between sequence, structure, and substrate specificity in GT-Bs. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is further scope for improvement in training machine learning models to establish interpretable relationships between sequence, structure, and substrate specificity in GT-Bs. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation and call for further improvement are stated equivalently in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Drones provide a cost-effective source of training data by capturing high-resolution orthophotos of tree mortality events at sub-centimeter resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Low altitude observation platforms provide a cost-effective source of training data by capturing high-resolution orthophotos of overstory tree mortality events. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes 'drones' to 'low altitude observation platforms' and specifies 'overstory' events, clarifying but not changing the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deadtrees.earth platform hosts more than a thousand centimeter-resolution orthophotos covering over 300,000 ha, with more than 58,000 ha fully annotated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deadtrees.earth platform hosts more than two thousand centimeter-resolution orthophotos, covering over 1,000,000 ha, with more than 58,000 ha manually annotated with live/dead tree classifications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports substantially larger dataset counts and area coverage, revising the numeric claims and broadening the platform's reported scope while increasing the asserted scale.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset from deadtrees.earth will serve as a foundation for a global initiative to gather comprehensive reference data on tree mortality. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This community-sourced dataset can serve as a comprehensive reference to uncover tree mortality patterns from local to global scales. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the dataset as a community-sourced comprehensive reference for uncovering patterns at multiple scales, clarifying the intended role without changing geographic scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of Earth observation data and machine learning will help uncover tree mortality patterns from local to global scales. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Earth observation data combined with supervised machine learning offers a promising approach to map overstory tree mortality consistently over space and time. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the conclusion as a promising approach to consistently map overstory mortality over space and time and expresses higher certainty, amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The open and interactive nature of deadtrees.earth aims to continuously increase our capacity to uncover and understand tree mortality patterns. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The open nature of deadtrees.earth will continuously increase our capacity to uncover and understand tree mortality dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the open-platform claim but omits 'interactive'; the intended outcome—increasing capacity to understand mortality dynamics—is preserved and slightly clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Flexible machine learning approaches are effective in filtering a subset of relevant confounders to mitigate selection bias in causal mediation analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Flexible machine learning approaches can effectively filter a subset of relevant confounders in causal mediation analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the methodological result with equivalent meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a gap in determining the best strategy for confounding selection in causal mediation analyses when encountering high-dimensional confounders. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a gap in determining the best strategy for confounding selection in causal mediation analyses with high-dimensional confounders. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is expressed equivalently in the publication, preserving intent and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study exemplifies the impact of confounding selection methods on causal target parameter estimation in the context of the human microbiome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study exemplifies the impact of confounding selection methods on causal target parameter estimation in the context of the human microbiome. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content of the example is unchanged, but the publication reports the claim with lower certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The discussed methods provide methodological implications that guide real-world applications and motivate future advancements in the analysis of the gut-brain axis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The discussed methods have practical implications for real-world applications and motivate future advancements in the field of causal mediation analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the application from the specific 'gut-brain axis' to the broader field of causal mediation analysis and expresses greater certainty about practical implications, revising and broadening the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Connections between omic layers can be discovered through a combination of machine learning and model interpretation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Connections between molecules in different omic layers were discovered through a combination of machine learning and model interpretation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical: both claim discovery of cross-omic connections using ML + model interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model interpretation values connecting proteins to metabolites are valid experimentally and reveal largely new connections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Proteins discovered to control citrate were mapped onto known genetic and metabolic networks, revealing that these protein regulators are novel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication provides a more specific example (citrate) and mapping to networks, narrowing the general preprint claim while preserving experimental novelty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clustering the magnitudes of protein control over all metabolites enabled prediction of five gene functions, each of which was validated experimentally. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clustering the magnitudes of protein control over all metabolites enabled the prediction of five gene functions, each of which was validated experimentally. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and content are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We accurately predicted that two uncharacterized genes in yeast modulate mitochondrial translation, YJR120W and YLD157C. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two uncharacterized genes, YJR120W and YDL157C, were accurately predicted to modulate mitochondrial translation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same predictive result but the second gene identifier differs (YLD157C -> YDL157C) in the publication, indicating a revision/correction of the gene name.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work demonstrates that multi-omic analysis with machine learning (MIMaL) reveals new insight that was not possible using existing methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this broad methodological-superiority claim; instead it focuses on specific discovered functions and examples.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Functions for three incompletely characterized genes were also predicted and validated, including SDH9, ISC1 and FMP52. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim (prediction/validation of three additional gene functions) does not appear in the preprint and is newly reported in the paper.
