alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CoVAMPnet framework quantifies changes in the conformational distribution and dynamics of disordered biomolecules in the presence of small organic drug candidates. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CoVAMPnet method is broadly applicable to study the effects of drug candidates on the conformational behavior of intrinsically disordered biomolecules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication emphasizes broad applicability of CoVAMPnet to study drug effects on disordered proteins; this clarifies/extends the preprint's methodological contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both TMP and SPA preserved more structured conformations of Aβ42 by interacting non-specifically with charged residues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both TMP and SPA preserved more structured conformations of Aβ42 by interacting nonspecifically with charged residues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result is essentially identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SPA impacted Aβ42 more than TMP, protecting α-helices and suppressing the formation of aggregation-prone β-strands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SPA impacted Aβ42 more than TMP, protecting α-helices and suppressing the formation of aggregation-prone β-strands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats this experimental finding without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental biophysical analyses showed only mild effects of TMP/SPA on Aβ42, and activity enhancement by the endogenous metabolization of TMP into SPA. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental biophysical analyses showed only mild effects of TMP/SPA on Aβ42 and activity enhancement by the endogenous metabolization of TMP into SPA. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental observation is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CoVAMPnet method is broadly applicable to study the effects of drug candidates on the conformational behavior of intrinsically disordered biomolecules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CoVAMPnet method is broadly applicable to study the effects of drug candidates on the conformational behavior of intrinsically disordered biomolecules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This methodological claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most recent advances in machine learning applied to a SUNG dataset succeed in unraveling the complex vocal repertoire of the bonobo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advances in machine learning applied to a SUNG dataset successfully unravel the complex vocal repertoire of the bonobo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is restated with equivalent meaning in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classification performance is affected by the feature set, and the most informative features have been identified. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classification performance is affected by the feature set used in the analysis of bonobo calls. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication frames the same observation more directly as a conclusion about feature-set effects; content is the same though wording differs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data leakage in the evaluation of classification performance needs to be addressed to avoid misleading interpretations. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Addressing data leakage in the evaluation of classification performance is essential to avoid misleading interpretations. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is expressed equivalently in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classifiers generate parsimonious data descriptions that help to understand the clustering of the bonobo acoustic space. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this more interpretive claim about parsimonious descriptions and clustering interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several practical approaches have been identified that are generalizable to any other animal communication system. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several practical approaches identified in this study are generalizable to any other animal communication system. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution/generalizability claim appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Functional connectivity indices demonstrate high sensitivity to task conditions in patients with minor ischemic strokes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Functional connectivity indices demonstrate high sensitivity to task conditions in patients with minor ischemic strokes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are negligible differences in brain network connectivity between patients with minor ischemic strokes and matched controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are negligible differences in brain network connectivity between patients with minor ischemic strokes and matched controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with minor ischemic strokes show significantly stronger synchronization between a temporal node and a sensorimotor node across conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with minor ischemic strokes show significantly stronger synchronization between a temporal node and a sensorimotor node across conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical finding in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOT task performance and cognitive tests reveal no significant group differences between patients and controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cognitive tests and MOT task performance reveal no significant group differences between patients and controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same negative result reported in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest relatively small brain network-level disturbances after clinically mild strokes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest relatively small brain network-level disturbances after clinically mild strokes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stable phase schizophrenia is characterized by altered patterning in tryptophan catabolites (TRYCATs) and memory impairments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lowered HR-QoL in schizophrenia is strongly associated with noxious TRYCATs, mediated by impairments in episodic/semantic memory and schizophrenia phenomenology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication frames the association in terms of lowered HR-QoL and mediation by memory impairments; this clarifies the preprint's general characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The total health-related quality of life (HR-QoL) score is best predicted by fibromyalgia and chronic fatigue syndrome rating scale (FF), Hamilton Anxiety Rating Scale (HAMA), and Scale for the Assessment of Negative Symptoms (SANS) scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The total HR-QoL score in schizophrenia patients is best predicted by HAMA, FF, HAMD, and psychosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Predictor set differs between versions (publication replaces SANS with HAMD and adds psychosis), indicating a revision of the predictor variables.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TRYCATs and episodic/semantic memory impairments have specific indirect effects on psychological, social, and environmental domains of HR-QoL, mediated by DAPS symptoms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 64.3% of the variance in PHEMN / DAPS symptoms is explained by TRYCAT patterning and episodic/semantic memory impairments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication quantifies how TRYCATs and memory impairments explain variance in symptoms (PHEMN/DAPS) rather than directly stating indirect effects on HR-QoL domains, shifting focus to symptom variance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lowered HR-QoL in schizophrenia is strongly predicted by noxious TRYCATs, impairments in episodic and semantic memory, and DAPS symptoms, particularly physio-somatic symptoms and anxiety. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lowered HR-QoL in schizophrenia is strongly associated with noxious TRYCATs, mediated by impairments in episodic/semantic memory and schizophrenia phenomenology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes prediction as association and emphasizes mediation by memory impairments and phenomenology, clarifying the preprint's predictors.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Mucosal activation of the TRYCAT pathway combined with a deficit in natural IgM isotype antibodies to TRYCATs determines cognitive impairments and DAPS/PHEMN symptoms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This mechanistic claim (mucosal activation + IgM deficit determining impairments) is new in the publication and has no counterpart in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAlight is a machine learning model developed to efficiently and sensitively predict subcellular localizations of mRNAs and lncRNAs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAlight is a machine learning model that identifies nucleotide k-mers contributing to the subcellular localizations of mRNAs and lncRNAs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the model contribution while emphasizing the k-mer features it identifies, clarifying the mechanism without changing the claim scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The embedded Tree SHAP algorithm in RNAlight reveals distinct key sequence features and their associated RBPs for subcellular localizations of mRNAs or lncRNAs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different types of sequence features and their associated RBPs were uncovered for lncRNAs and mRNAs with distinct subcellular localizations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits and frames the Tree SHAP findings more concretely by RNA class (lncRNA vs mRNA) and associated RBPs, clarifying the original preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAlight is successfully extended for the subcellular localization prediction of additional types of noncoding RNAs, such as circular RNAs, suggesting its generality in RNA subcellular localization prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAlight can be extended to predict the subcellular localizations of other types of RNAs, including snRNAs, snoRNAs, and different circular RNA transcripts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the extension claim to include additional RNA types beyond circular RNAs, broadening the scope while retaining the same conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Passive visual stimulation affects the performance of a subsequent task carried out in the same portion of visual space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Passive visual stimulation affects the performance of a subsequent task carried out in the same portion of visual space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim, with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The outcome of performance can either be enhanced or deteriorated based on the participants’ level of cognitive load and arousal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Under high arousal conditions, participants experienced a performance decrement that correlated with their reported subjective level of fatigue. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed bidirectional effects depending on load/arousal; the publication reports only decrements under high arousal and links them to subjective fatigue, thus narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Repeated stimulation of neural networks leads to altered functional performance, which may contribute to the development of global mental fatigue. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Repeated stimulation of neural networks under high-arousal conditions leads to altered functional performance, which may contribute to the development of global cognitive fatigue. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the conclusion but specifies that effects occur under high-arousal conditions and uses 'cognitive' rather than 'mental', clarifying and narrowing the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Base editors enable conversion of C•G into T•A base pairs and vice versa on genomic DNA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Base editors enable the transition of C•G into T•A base pairs and vice versa on genomic DNA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the technical capability of base editors with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a broad variation in editing efficiencies of base editors on different genomic loci. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of base editors has been hampered by a broad variation in editing efficiencies on different genomic loci. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same observation as a practical limitation and reduces the stated certainty, clarifying and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BE-DICT is an attention-based deep learning algorithm capable of predicting base editing outcomes with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BE-DICT is an attention-based deep learning algorithm capable of predicting base editing outcomes with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim directly with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BE-DICT can be trained on any novel base editor variant, facilitating the application of base editing for research and therapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BE-DICT can be trained on any novel base editor variant, facilitating the application of base editing for research and therapy. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same practical conclusion about BE-DICT's trainability and potential applications with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cellpose is a generalist, deep learning-based segmentation method that can precisely segment cells from a wide range of image types. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cellpose is a generalist, deep learning-based segmentation method that can precisely segment cells from a wide range of image types without requiring model retraining or parameter adjustments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the generalist segmentation claim and explicitly adds that it does not require retraining or parameter adjustments, clarifying practical usability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cellpose does not require model retraining or parameter adjustments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cellpose is a generalist, deep learning-based segmentation method that can precisely segment cells from a wide range of image types without requiring model retraining or parameter adjustments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication incorporates this exact conclusion into a compound claim; the practical claim about no retraining or parameter tuning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cellpose was trained on a new dataset of highly-varied images of cells, containing over 70,000 segmented objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cellpose was trained on a new dataset of highly varied images of cells, containing over 70,000 segmented objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this training-dataset/result claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A 3D extension of Cellpose reuses the 2D model and does not require 3D-labelled data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A three-dimensional (3D) extension of Cellpose reuses the two-dimensional (2D) model and does not require 3D-labeled data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the 3D extension claim with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Periodically retraining the model on community-contributed data will ensure that Cellpose improves constantly. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Periodically retraining the model on community-contributed data will ensure that Cellpose improves constantly. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion about continual improvement through community-contributed retraining with the same framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration approach of SpaCell outperforms the use of gene count alone or imaging data alone in creating deep learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration approach of SpaCell outperforms the use of gene-count data alone or imaging data alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the comparative performance claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SpaCell enables the integration of millions of pixel intensity values with thousands of gene expression measurements. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SpaCell enables the integration of millions of pixel intensity values with thousands of gene expression measurements. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SpaCell achieves high resolution and accuracy in identifying cell types and predicting labels of tissue images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SpaCell achieves high resolution and accuracy in identifying cell types and predicting labels of tissue images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this performance claim unchanged in wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Kranz syndrome is characterized by specific leaf anatomical and functional traits in species that utilize C 4 photosynthesis.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state or reproduce a claim about 'Kranz syndrome' characterization; the concept is absent from the listed published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis using Self-Organizing Maps (SOM) identified candidate genes that enable differentiation of key anatomical traits related to mesophyll and bundle sheath cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis allowed us to identify a set of genes as potential enablers of key anatomical trait differentiation related to bundle sheath cell size, vein density, and the interface between mesophyll and bundle sheath cells.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the gene-identification result with more detail on the specific traits considered (adds vein density and interface), clarifying and slightly broadening the trait scope while preserving the original finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A small subset of genes was found to co-displace with changes in the area of bundle sheath cells, indicating their significant role in the evolution of Kranz anatomy in grasses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified genes that displaced together with the adjustment of the bundle sheath cell area suggesting a possible role in the evolution of this distinctive leaf anatomical trait.
alignments.rationale: The published phrasing closely matches the preprint observation about co-displacement with bundle sheath cell area; the publication couches the evolutionary implication more cautiously ('suggesting a possible role'), hence a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study utilized a novel machine learning methodology to discover key genes involved in Kranz differentiation.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not emphasize or claim a novel machine-learning methodology for discovering genes, so the methodological novelty claim is not represented among the provided publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Co-Fractionation Mass Spectrometry (CFMS) enables the discovery of protein complexes and the systems-level analyses of multimer dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed integrative analysis method leverages cross-kingdom evolutionary conservation and high-resolution SEC profile data to create reliable benchmark complexes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution in terms of a specific integrative analysis method producing reliable benchmark complexes, narrowing the general CFMS-enablement claim into a concrete methodological outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A major challenge in CFMS analyses is to conduct validation experiments at scale and develop precise methods to evaluate performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting benchmark complexes are supported by statistical significance and consistent sizes between calculated and measured apparent masses.
alignments.rationale: The preprint highlights a validation challenge, while the publication presents benchmark complexes with statistical support, effectively addressing and reframing the challenge as a resolved validation outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine learning-based 'small world' data analysis method identifies relatively rare instances of fully assembled complexes and stable CORUM subcomplexes.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not mention the specific 'small world' ML method or the identification of CORUM subcomplexes, so this specific methodological/result claim is not reflected in the provided publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated gold standards are expected to serve as improved benchmarks to assess the overall reliability of CFMS-based protein complex composition predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed integrative analysis method leverages cross-kingdom evolutionary conservation and high-resolution SEC profile data to create reliable benchmark complexes.
alignments.rationale: The publication operationalizes the preprint's expected gold standards by describing the integrative method that creates reliable benchmark complexes; the claim is presented with stronger methodological detail and support.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictability of protein variant effects strongly depends on structural characteristics such as buriedness, number of contact residues, proximity to the active site, and presence of secondary structure elements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictability of variant effect prediction models strongly depends on structural characteristics such as buriedness, number of contact residues, proximity to the active site, and presence of secondary structure elements.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same dependency on structural characteristics with essentially identical content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The same dependencies on structural characteristics were found in various single mutation enzyme variant datasets, with effect directions being specific to the assay.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dependencies of predictability on structural characteristics were also observed in several single mutation enzyme variant datasets, indicating dataset-specific variations.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same observation across single-mutation enzyme datasets and note dataset-specific variations; wording differs slightly but the substance is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are specific structure and function determinants that are insufficiently accounted for by popular existing approaches to variant effect prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current machine learning algorithms inadequately account for specific structure and function determinants that influence predictability.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the statement by attributing the insufficiency specifically to current machine learning algorithms and does so with higher asserted certainty, amplifying the concern.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant improvements can be made to variant effect prediction models by exploring new inductive biases and leveraging different data modalities of protein variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Improvements to variant effect prediction models can be achieved by exploring new inductive biases and leveraging different data modalities of protein variants.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same recommendation for future model improvement with very similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WDR91 regulates endosomal phosphatidylinositol 3-phosphate levels and plays vital roles in endosome fusion, recycling, and transport.
alignments.pub_claim_text: WDR91 regulates early-to-late endosome conversion and plays vital roles in endosome fusion, recycling, and transport.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes regulation of early-to-late endosome conversion rather than specifically stating PI3P level regulation; the functional roles are retained but the mechanistic description is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hit compound 1 binds selectively to WDR91 with a K D of 6 ± 2 μM.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Screening of predicted compounds identified a WDR91 selective compound 1, with a KD of 6 ± 2 μM by surface plasmon resonance.
alignments.rationale: The binding affinity and selectivity for compound 1 are reported with the same KD and are confirmed by the publication (SPR specified), so the claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning-assisted structure activity relationship validated the chemotype of compound 1 and led to the discovery of covalent analogs 18 and 19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The discovery of 1, 18, and 19, along with accompanying structure-activity relationship and co-crystal structures, provides valuable insights for designing potent and selective chemical tools against WDR91.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the discovery of analogs 18 and 19 and reports supporting SAR and co-crystal structures, thereby clarifying and substantiating the preprint's ML-assisted SAR-driven discovery claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The discovery of compounds 1, 18, and 19, along with accompanying SAR and co-crystal structures, will provide valuable insights for designing more potent and selective compounds against WDR91.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The discovery of 1, 18, and 19, along with accompanying structure-activity relationship and co-crystal structures, provides valuable insights for designing potent and selective chemical tools against WDR91.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution: the discoveries plus SAR and structures offer insights for designing potent, selective tools against WDR91.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tool MarCNV has been developed and evaluated for interpreting copy number variants (CNVs) using records from the ClinVar database.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tool MarCNV has been developed and evaluated for interpreting copy number variants (CNVs) using records from the ClinVar database.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same development and evaluation claim for MarCNV with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning-based tools like ISV show potential for fully automated predictions of CNV pathogenicity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning-based tools like ISV show promise for fully automated predictions in CNV classification.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same conclusion about ML tools' potential/promise for automated CNV classification, with similar hedging and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combined approach of using MarCNV and ISV can reduce the number of uncertain classifications and reveal potentially incorrect classifications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined approach of MarCNV and ISV can reduce the number of uncertain classifications and reveal potentially incorrect classifications.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the combined-approach result verbatim, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SATINN achieves an accuracy of 94.2% in classifying nuclei from seminiferous tubules into 7 distinct cell types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SATINN achieves an accuracy of 81.7% in classifying nuclei from seminiferous tubules into seven distinct cell types.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same claim but with a substantially lower accuracy value, so the result was revised and the confidence is effectively tempered by the reduced performance metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second-level tubule CNN places seminiferous tubules into one of 7 distinct tubule stages with an accuracy of 90.4%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The second-level tubule CNN places seminiferous tubules into one of 12 distinct tubule stages with 57.3% direct accuracy and 94.9% within ±1 stage.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the staging scheme (7 → 12) and reports different accuracy metrics (lower direct accuracy but high ±1-stage), so the claim was revised and presented more conservatively.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifiers and derived statistics from SATINN can be used to rapidly and precisely describe pathology in testis histology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifiers and derived statistics from SATINN can be used to rapidly and precisely describe pathology in testis histology.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion with the same scope and level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of SATINN represents a significant contribution to the field of testis histology by enabling standardized, digital image analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the feasibility and potential of using computer-assisted analysis for testis histology.
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the strength of the contribution into a demonstration of feasibility and potential, thus clarifying and somewhat tempering the original, stronger claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of high density complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor (CMOS) MEAs greatly increases the spatiotemporal resolution, enabling sub-cellular readout and stimulation of neurons in defined neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of high density complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor (CMOS) MEAs greatly increases the spatial resolution, enabling sub-cellular readout and stimulation of neurons in defined neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that CMOS MEAs increase resolution and enable sub-cellular readout, but drops explicit mention of temporal resolution, narrowing the original phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide an impedance-based method to visualize the exact location of the microstructures on the MEA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The impedance-based method provided can visualize the exact location of the microstructures on the MEA and can confine axonal growth within the PDMS microstructures.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the impedance-visualization claim but reports the overall claim with a more cautious (medium) confidence and adds additional functionality; the original visualization claim itself is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method can confine axonal growth within the PDMS microstructures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The impedance-based method provided can visualize the exact location of the microstructures on the MEA and can confine axonal growth within the PDMS microstructures.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes this confinement result but reports it with lower certainty (medium), so the content is unchanged but the expressed confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The high spatiotemporal resolution of the CMOS MEA enabled us to show that we can guide action potentials using the unidirectional topology of our circular multi-node microstructure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high spatial resolution of the CMOS MEA enabled the demonstration that action potentials follow the unidirectional topology of the circular multi-node microstructure.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same demonstration but omits explicit temporal claims (spatiotemporal → spatial) and slightly reframes the wording; thus the claim is revised and narrowed in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The World Health Organization recommends using Generalized Additive Models for Location, Scale, and Shape (GAMLSS) to estimate population reference ranges across age and sex.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate or cite a WHO recommendation; this preprint claim is absent from the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fitting GAMLSS models requires large, generalizable sample sizes for accurate estimation of extreme quantiles.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly assert this requirement; the specific claim about sample-size needs for extreme quantiles is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose distributed GAMLSS (dGAMLSS), a distributed algorithm that can fit GAMLSS models across multiple sites without sharing patient-level data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose distributed GAMLSS (dGAMLSS), a distributed algorithm that can fit GAMLSS models across multiple sites without sharing patient-level data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological contribution verbatim in scope and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the effectiveness of dGAMLSS in constructing population reference charts across clinical, genomics, and neuroimaging settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: dGAMLSS is effective in constructing population reference charts across clinical, genomics, and neuroimaging settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same demonstrated effectiveness across the same application domains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SYN-AI constructed a library of 10 million genes that was reduced to three structurally functional 14-3-3 docking genes by applying natural selection protocols.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SYN-AI constructed a library of 10 million genes, which was reduced to three structurally functional 14-3-3 docking genes through natural selection protocols.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this experimental result with the same numbers and process.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DNA secondary code allows identification of genomic building blocks across an orthologous sequence space comprising multiple genomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not include this specific assertion about a DNA secondary code identifying genomic building blocks, so the preprint claim does not appear in the published set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We report the first 14-3-3 docking genes to be written from scratch.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study reports the first 14-3-3 docking genes to be written from scratch.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly repeats this contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conservation of allosteric communications were confirmed utilizing elastic and anisotropic network models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conservation of allosteric communications in the 14-3-3 ζ amphipathic groove was confirmed using elastic and anisotropic network models.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the same conservation using the same models but specifies the structural locus (14-3-3 ζ amphipathic groove), thus clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulated retinal activations elicited with our ANN-based approach are considerably sharper compared to the conventional method used in existing devices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANN-based NAS approach produces sharper retinal activations than the conventional unipolar stimulation strategy.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same comparative claim about sharper activations using the ANN approach with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Stimulus Generator Network (SGN) finds multipolar stimulation patterns that are tuned to a specific retina, thus providing patient-specific stimuli.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our novel protocol provides a method for efficient and personalized retinal stimulation, which may improve the visual experience and quality of life of retinal prosthesis users.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the SGN's personalized stimulation capability in a slightly broader protocol-level framing but preserves the claim of patient-specific stimuli and its intended benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SGN can output stimulation patterns at a very high rate due to its small computational cost.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANN strategy produced equivalent results to AMI while being three orders of magnitude more computationally efficient.
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies and contextualizes the computational efficiency (3 orders of magnitude and equivalence to AMI), which clarifies and supports the preprint's claim about high-rate output due to low cost.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our novel protocol opens the door to personalized multipolar retinal stimulation, which may improve the visual experience and quality of life of retinal prosthesis users.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our novel protocol provides a method for efficient and personalized retinal stimulation, which may improve the visual experience and quality of life of retinal prosthesis users.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same cautious conclusion about potential quality-of-life improvements from personalized stimulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning can predict point mutations, molecular tumor subtypes and immune-related gene expression signatures directly from routine histological images of tumor tissue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning can consistently infer a wide range of genetic mutations, molecular tumor subtypes, gene expression signatures, and standard pathology biomarkers directly from routine histology.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core result but in broader terms (a wider range of genetic and pathology biomarkers) and emphasizes consistency; this is a clarification/expansion rather than a contradiction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A single deep learning algorithm can predict clinically actionable alterations from routine histology data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A single deep learning algorithm can be trained to predict a wide range of molecular alterations from routine, paraffin-embedded histology slides stained with hematoxylin and eosin.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the claim that a single algorithm can predict molecular alterations but specifies slide type and expands to a wider range of alterations; the emphasis on "clinically actionable" is not explicitly retained, so this is a clarification/broadened statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method can be implemented on mobile hardware, potentially enabling point-of-care diagnostics for personalized cancer treatment in individual patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method can be implemented on mobile hardware, potentially enabling point-of-care diagnostics for personalized cancer treatment.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same implementation and potential point-of-care diagnostic conclusion with comparable confidence and scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictions made by the deep learning model generalize to other populations and are spatially resolved.
alignments.rationale: This generalization/spatial-resolution claim was not present in the preprint and appears as a new result in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach could elucidate and quantify genotype–phenotype links in cancer.
alignments.rationale: The idea of elucidating or quantifying genotype–phenotype links is an additional concluding claim not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our deep learning approach achieves an accuracy of 90.2% when classifying metastatic origin of whole slide images into three distinct classes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach achieves a classification accuracy of 90.2% in determining metastatic origin of whole slide images from a held-out test set.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric accuracy on a held-out test set; wording is equivalent and confidence remains high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of our approach compares favorably to an established clinical benchmark by three board-certified pathologists whose accuracies ranged from 90.2% to 94.1%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification accuracy of our approach compares favorably to an established clinical benchmark by three board-certified pathologists whose accuracies ranged from 90.2% to 94.1%.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the direct comparison to pathologist benchmark accuracies with the same numbers and framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach illustrates the potential impact of deep learning systems to guide pathological and clinical determination of the metastatic origin of secondary liver cancers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We illustrate the potential impact of deep learning systems to leverage morphological and structural features of H&E-stained tissue sections.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution to focusing on leveraging morphological/structural features of H&E tissue rather than explicitly claiming guidance of pathological/clinical determination, reducing the clinical claim and toning the implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based method achieves super-resolution in fluorescence microscopy without requiring numerical models of the imaging process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep-learning-enabled super-resolution approach improves the resolution of wide-field images to match that of high-numerical-aperture objectives.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the core super-resolution result but omits the explicit methodological claim about not requiring numerical models, so the statement is revised to focus on the outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This method can super-resolve wide-field images acquired with low numerical aperture objective lenses to match the resolution of high numerical aperture objectives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep-learning-enabled super-resolution approach improves the resolution of wide-field images to match that of high-numerical-aperture objectives.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this specific result directly and with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diffraction-limited confocal microscopy images can be transformed into super-resolved fluorescence images using the same framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework demonstrates cross-modality super-resolution by transforming confocal microscopy images to match the resolution of a stimulated emission depletion (STED) microscope.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the target modality (STED) when describing confocal-to-super-resolution transformations, clarifying the original cross-modality claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep network outputs super-resolution images rapidly, without any iterations or parameter search.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep network outputs super-resolved images rapidly, without any iterations or parameter search.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the rapid, non-iterative inference claim but lists it with medium rather than high certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method works for types of samples that it was not specifically trained for.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state or provide an explicit claim about out-of-training-sample generalization, so this claim from the preprint is not represented in the published claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Total internal reflection fluorescence (TIRF) microscopy images can be transformed to match the results obtained with a TIRF-based structured illumination microscope.
alignments.rationale: This specific TIRF-to-structured-illumination transformation is a new, modality-specific result present in the publication but not in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach could serve to democratize super-resolution imaging.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a broader impact claim about democratization that was not asserted in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: YAMDA is a highly scalable motif discovery software package built on Pytorch.
alignments.pub_claim_text: YAMDA is a highly scalable motif discovery software package that takes linear time to find motifs.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim of high scalability; wording differs slightly (omitting explicit mention of PyTorch) but the core contribution is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: YAMDA takes linear time to find motifs as accurately as MEME.
alignments.pub_claim_text: YAMDA achieves the same accuracy in motif discovery as MEME while completing the task in seconds or minutes.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms equivalent accuracy to MEME and rapid completion; this matches the preprint claim that linear-time search attains MEME-level accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: YAMDA completes motif discovery in seconds or minutes, resulting in speedups over a thousandfold compared to MEME.
alignments.pub_claim_text: YAMDA provides speedups over a thousandfold compared to MEME for large datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly quantifies the large speedups and runtime, which directly corresponds to the preprint's performance claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Theta oscillations and preferential firing of single neurons at theta phase occur during memory maintenance in the medial temporal lobe.
alignments.pub_claim_text: During memory maintenance, theta oscillations and preferential firing of single neurons to theta phase are observed in the medial temporal lobe.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical observation about theta oscillations and phase-preferential firing during memory maintenance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The phase of firing is related to item position within a sequence, but does not match item order.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The firing order of neurons does not match the item order during the working-memory task.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the negative result about mismatch between firing order and item order but omits the positive phrasing about phase relating to item position, narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recurrent neural networks (RNNs) generate theta oscillations during memory maintenance, similar to recorded neural activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a mechanistic link between phase order, stimulus timing, and oscillation frequency in both biological and artificial neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the computational result to a mechanistic link across biological and artificial networks, which clarifies and broadens the preprint's statement about RNNs producing similar oscillations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a mechanistic link between phase order, stimulus timing, and oscillation frequency, confirmed in neural recordings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a mechanistic link between phase order, stimulus timing, and oscillation frequency in both biological and artificial neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the mechanistic link and extends it to include artificial networks; this clarifies and broadens the original mechanistic claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings challenge a long-held theory about the functional role of spiking and oscillations in sequence memory.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evidence supports the role of phase of firing in working-memory processing in both biological and artificial neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the interpretation: rather than claiming a challenge to prior theory, it presents evidence supporting a role for phase of firing, a substantive revision with higher stated confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents a comprehensive and novel suite of myoelectric prosthesis control evaluation metrics that facilitate better analysis of device movement details. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a comprehensive and novel suite of myoelectric prosthesis control evaluation metrics that better facilitates analysis of device movement details. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical and the contribution is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analysis of the new metrics identified 16 instances of a user-facing problem known as the 'limb position effect'. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis identified 16 instances of a user-facing problem known as the 'limb position effect'. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported result (16 instances) is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based controller (RCNN-TL) performed the same as or significantly better than the commonly used controller (LDA) in four instances of the limb position effect. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning-based controller (RCNN-TL) performed the same as or significantly better than the commonly used controller (LDA) in four instances of the limb position effect. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact same performance claim about RCNN-TL vs LDA is present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transfer learning can minimize user training burden in the context of myoelectric prosthesis control. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer learning can minimize user training burden. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shortens the wording by dropping the explicit device context, making the conclusion more general while retaining the same intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method makes a significant improvement over a single-sequence-based predictor SSCpred with 15% improvement in the F1-score for the independent CASP14-FM test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method makes a significant improvement over a single-sequence-based predictor SSCpred with 15% improvement in the F1-score for the independent CASP14-FM test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical quantitative performance claim retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method outperforms evolutionary-profile-based methods TrRosetta and SPOT-Contact with 48.7% and 48.5% respective improvement in the F1-score on the proteins without homologs in the independent SPOT-2018 set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method outperforms evolutionary-profile-based methods trRosetta and SPOT-Contact with 48.7% and 48.5% respective improvement in the F1-score on the proteins without homologs in the independent SPOT-2018 set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance numbers and context are unchanged (only minor capitalization difference).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method provides a much faster and reasonably accurate alternative to evolution-based methods, useful for large-scale prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method provides a much faster and reasonably accurate alternative to evolution-based methods, useful for large-scale prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about speed and practical utility is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CatOpt outperformed existing predictors of enzyme optimal pH with an RMSE of 0.833 and R2 of 0.479. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CatOpt outperformed existing predictors of enzyme optimal pH with an RMSE of 0.833 and R² of 0.479. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numerical performance metrics and comparative claim are identical (formatting of R^2 differs only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CatOpt effectively classifies acidic and alkaline enzymes and predicts enzyme optimal pH shifts caused by point mutations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CatOpt effectively classifies acidophilic and alkaliphilic enzymes and predicts enzyme optimal pH shifts caused by point mutations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Terminology differs (acidic/alkaline vs acidophilic/alkaliphilic) but the meaning and claim are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A single point mutation designed with the guidance of CatOpt enhanced the activity of Pyrococcus horikoshii diacetylchitobiose deacetylase at low pH by approximately 7%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A single point mutation designed with the guidance of CatOpt enhanced the activity of Pyrococcus horikoshii diacetylchitobiose deacetylase at low pH by approximately 7%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific experimental enhancement and magnitude are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CatOpt is a promising in-silico enzyme design tool for pH-dependent enzyme activities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CatOpt is a promising in silico enzyme design tool for pH-dependent enzyme activities. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication reports a lower certainty for this conclusion, indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProtNote is a multimodal deep learning model that enables both supervised and zero-shot protein function prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProtNote is a multimodal deep-learning model that enables both supervised and zero-shot protein-function prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Core contribution statement is the same; differences are minor wording/formatting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProtNote maintains near state-of-the-art performance for annotations in its train set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProtNote maintains near state-of-the-art performance for annotations in its training set and generalizes to unseen and novel functions in zero-shot test settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication merges the in-training performance claim with generalization results (previous P2), broadening the single preprint claim into a combined statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProtNote generalizes to unseen and novel functions in zero-shot test settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProtNote maintains near state-of-the-art performance for annotations in its training set and generalizes to unseen and novel functions in zero-shot test settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The generalization claim is preserved verbatim within the combined publication claim describing both training and zero-shot performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProtNote will enhance protein function discovery by allowing scientists to use free text inputs without restriction to predefined labels. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProtNote captures nuanced sequence-function relationships that unlock a range of biological use cases inaccessible to prior models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from the specific free-text input capability to a broader claim about capturing nuanced relationships and enabling varied use cases, altering the focus of the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MOVE model increases the number of multi-omic profiles by 32.7%, generating a complete DepMap for 1,523 cancer cell lines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unsupervised deep learning model MOSA increases the number of multi-omic profiles by 32.7%, generating a complete DepMap for 1523 cancer cell lines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Quantitative result is identical; only the model name/formatting changed (MOVE -> MOSA) and minor formatting of the cell line count.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The synthetically enhanced data from MOVE increases statistical power and uncovers less studied mechanisms associated with drug resistance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The synthetically enhanced data from MOSA increases statistical power and uncovers less studied mechanisms associated with drug resistance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content of the result is the same but the publication expresses higher certainty about this effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOVE reveals multi-omic features essential for cell clustering and biomarker identification related to drug and gene dependencies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MOSA reveals multi-omic features essential for cell clustering and biomarker identification related to drug and gene dependencies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same substantive claim about features for clustering and biomarker identification; naming/formatting differs only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The understanding gained from MOVE is crucial for developing effective strategies in prioritizing cancer targets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating diverse types of biological data is essential for a holistic understanding of cancer biology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces a MOVE-specific conclusion about target prioritization with a more general statement about data integration and holistic understanding, changing the focus and scope of the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A good scoring function for docking remains elusive despite numerous experiments with innovative scoring functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A good scoring function for protein-protein docking remains elusive despite many innovative scoring functions being proposed.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with essentially identical wording and emphasis, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models offer alternatives to using explicit empirical or mathematical functions for scoring protein-protein complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study performs a comprehensive survey of state-of-the-art scoring functions, including both classical and deep learning-based approaches.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's assertion about deep learning as an alternative is not explicitly asserted in the paper; the publication clarifies that deep learning approaches were included in the survey, which narrows the original general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study performs a comprehensive survey of the state-of-the-art scoring functions for protein-protein complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study performs a comprehensive survey of state-of-the-art scoring functions, including both classical and deep learning-based approaches.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same claim that the study is a comprehensive survey; the publication simply specifies the types of approaches surveyed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey evaluates the strengths and weaknesses of popular scoring methods to aid researchers in understanding progress in the field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of classical and deep learning-based approaches across seven datasets aids researchers in understanding progress in scoring functions for protein-protein docking.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more detail (e.g., datasets and method classes) but preserves the original intent that the evaluation helps researchers interpret progress.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SC2Spa outperformed other predictors and accurately detected tissue architecture from transcriptome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SC2Spa outperformed other predictors and accurately detected tissue architecture from transcriptome.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the result claim verbatim and with same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SC2Spa successfully mapped single cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) to Visium assay, enhancing the resolution for low resolution ST data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SC2Spa successfully mapped single cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) to Visium assay, enhancing the resolution for low resolution spatial transcriptomics data.
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated in the publication with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SC2Spa performs well for various ST technologies and is robust to spatial resolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SC2Spa performs well for various spatial transcriptomics technologies and is robust to spatial resolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this performance claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SC2Spa can suggest spatially variable genes that cannot be identified from previous approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SC2Spa can suggest spatially variable genes that cannot be identified from previous approaches.
alignments.rationale: The claim about discovering spatially variable genes is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SC2Spa is a robust and accurate approach to provide single cells with their spatial location and identify spatially meaningful genes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SC2Spa is a robust and accurate approach to provide single cells with their spatial location and identify spatially meaningful genes.
alignments.rationale: The summary/conclusion is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MultiPPIMI achieves an average AUROC of 0.837 in three cold-start scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MultiPPIMI achieves an average AUROC of 0.837 in three cold-start scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The numeric result is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MultiPPIMI achieves an AUROC of 0.994 in the random-split scenario.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MultiPPIMI achieves an AUROC of 0.994 in the random-split scenario.
alignments.rationale: The reported performance metric is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MultiPPIMI can assist molecular simulations in screening inhibitors of Keap1/Nrf2 PPI interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MultiPPIMI can assist molecular docking simulations in screening inhibitors of Keap1/Nrf2 PPI interactions.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'docking' simulations, making the application more specific while preserving the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method provides a promising way to screen PPI-targeted modulators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides a promising way to screen PPI-targeted modulators.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study predicts the assembly of essential proteins in bacteria using the deep learning protein folding algorithm Alphafold2.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We predicted and modeled 1402 interactions between essential proteins in bacteria.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a quantitative outcome (1402 interactions) and phrasing about prediction/modeling, clarifying the preprint's broader statement about using AlphaFold2.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis reveals previously unknown details about the assembly mechanisms of protein complexes, emphasizing the importance of specific structural features in their stability and function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analysis reveals previously unknown details about the assembly mechanisms of these complexes.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the analytical finding with similar scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several novel protein-protein interactions have been identified, providing new targets for drug development.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results presented here offer a promising approach to identify novel antibiotic targets.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broader claim about drug development is narrowed in the publication to antibiotic targets, and the publication frames this with somewhat lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work provides a framework for predicting the interactome of other bacteria.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work provides a framework for predicting the essential interactomes of bacteria.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'essential interactomes' (narrower) and presents the claim with higher certainty, amplifying confidence relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The S1000 corpus enables highly accurate recognition of species names with an F-score of 93.1%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The S1000 corpus enables highly accurate recognition of species names with an F-score of 93.1%.
alignments.rationale: The performance result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning and dictionary-based methods benefit from the S1000 corpus for species name recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The S1000 corpus is a comprehensive manual re-annotation and extension of the S800 corpus.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the corpus itself (its re-annotation/extension), which clarifies why methods would benefit, but it does not restate the preprint's explicit claim about both method classes benefiting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The poor results in species name recognition are primarily due to the lack of appropriate corpora.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-based methods for species name recognition have previously shown poor results due to a lack of appropriate corpora.
alignments.rationale: The preprint attributes poor results broadly to lack of corpora; the publication narrows this to deep learning-based methods specifically, revising and narrowing the original attribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The viRNAtrap pipeline allows for the identification of viral reads and assembly of viral contigs without alignment to known virus databases. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim restates the alignment-free/assembly-from-reads capability; this technical contribution in the preprint is absent from the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using viRNAtrap, unexpected and divergent viruses not previously implicated in cancer were uncovered. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The viRNAtrap pipeline uncovers expression of unexpected and divergent viruses that have not previously been implicated in cancer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about uncovering unexpected/divergent viruses with essentially the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The expression of human endogenous viruses identified by viRNAtrap is associated with poor overall survival in cancer patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The expression of human endogenous viruses is associated with poor overall survival. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the association between endogenous virus expression and poor overall survival with the same level of claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: viRNAtrap can be applied to explore viral expression across 14 cancer types from The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: viRNAtrap provides a way forward to study viral infections associated with different clinical conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution more generally (studying viral infections across clinical conditions) rather than the specific application to 14 TCGA cancer types, and the stated certainty is lower.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning generative approaches allow for the exploration of protein structure space beyond natural proteins. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning generative approaches allow for the exploration of protein structure space beyond natural proteins. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Crystal structures of 7 designs show a median RMSD of 0.6 Å compared to computational models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Crystal structures of seven designs generated by deep network hallucination are very similar to the computational models, with a median root mean square deviation of 0.6 angstroms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same numerical RMSD result and interpretation, differing only in phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three cryoEM structures of giant rings differ considerably from previously solved structures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three cryo-electron microscopy structures of giant 10-nanometer rings differ considerably from previously solved structures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a size descriptor (10-nm) but preserves the core result that the cryoEM structures differ substantially from prior structures.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate the potential for designing increasingly complex nanomachines and biomaterials using deep learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings pave the way for the design of increasingly complex components for nanomachines and biomaterials. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same forward-looking conclusion with slightly stronger phrasing about enabling design, hence the confidence is effectively amplified while the semantic content remains aligned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SPACE-GM is substantially more accurate in predicting patient outcomes than previous approaches for modeling spatial data using neighborhood cell-type compositions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The graph deep learning model was substantially more accurate in predicting patient outcomes than deep learning approaches that model spatial data based on local composition of cell types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim restates the performance advantage of the graph model over local composition–based approaches with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of SPACE-GM to 658 head-and-neck and colorectal human cancer samples identified spatial motifs associated with cancer recurrence and patient survival after immunotherapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spatial cellular-graph strategy identified spatial motifs associated with cancer recurrence and patient survival after treatment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core result about spatial motifs linked to recurrence and survival but removes the specific sample count and the explicit immunotherapy context, broadening the clinical scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computational interpretation of the disease-relevant microenvironments identified by SPACE-GM generates insights into the effect of spatial dispersion of tumor cells and granulocytes on patient prognosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Local graphs may aid in the analysis of disease-relevant motifs in histology samples characterized via spatial transcriptomics and other -omics techniques. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the interpretative utility of local graphs more generally for analyzing disease-relevant motifs, which aligns with the preprint's interpretative conclusion but with slightly different emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepPlnc identifies plant lncRNAs with high accuracy using sequence and structural properties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepPlnc identifies plant lncRNAs with high accuracy using sequence and structural properties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim regarding method and accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepPlnc achieved an accuracy of over 98% when tested across a large number of validated instances. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepPlnc achieved an accuracy of over 98% when tested across a large number of validated instances. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported accuracy and testing description are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: During benchmarking, DeepPlnc outperformed all compared tools by a significant margin of 4.6%-10.3%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepPlnc consistently outperformed all compared tools by a significant margin of 2.5%-4.6% in performance metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a substantially smaller performance margin than the preprint, representing a numerical revision and a narrowing of the claimed advantage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepPlnc is suggested to be more suitable for genome and transcriptome annotation purposes than existing tools. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepPlnc is suggested to be more suitable for genome and transcriptome annotation purposes than existing tools. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same cautious recommendation about suitability for annotation as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Colorectal poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (ADC) has a poor prognosis compared to well to moderately differentiated ADC. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Poorly differentiated colorectal adenocarcinoma has a poor prognosis compared to well to moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same clinical conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The frequency of poorly differentiated ADC is usually less than 5% among colorectal carcinomas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The frequency of poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma is usually less than 5% among colorectal carcinomas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The frequency statistic is repeated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models trained using a simple transfer learning method can classify poorly differentiated colorectal ADC from Whole Slide Images (WSIs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models trained using a simple transfer learning method can classify poorly differentiated colorectal adenocarcinoma from Whole Slide Images with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same classification capability and explicitly notes high accuracy, which aligns with the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models achieved receiver operator curve (ROC) area under the curves (AUCs) in the range of 0.94-0.98. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieved receiver operating characteristic curve area under the curves up to 0.95 on 1799 test cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the reported performance (AUC upper bound reduced to 0.95 and a specific test set size added), which narrows the previously reported range and reduces the claimed peak performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synchronized or desynchronized electrical activities in the brain can lead to neurodegenerative disorders such as Huntington’s disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synchronized or desynchronized electrical activities in the brain can contribute to neurodegenerative conditions like Huntington's disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same relationship but softens 'lead to' into 'contribute to', which clarifies and weakens the causal strength of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Hodgkin-Huxley model describes the propagation of action potentials in neurons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dynamic entrainment technique matches the outputs of the mechanistic Hodgkin-Huxley model.
