alignments.rationale: The preprint makes a general descriptive claim about the Hodgkin-Huxley model, whereas the publication uses the HH model as a reference point for evaluating a technique; the claim's focus is shifted and narrowed to an application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Dynamic Entrainment technique can maintain the system in its entrainment regime dynamically by applying deep learning approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dynamic entrainment technique matches the outputs of the mechanistic Hodgkin-Huxley model.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's tentative claim about maintaining entrainment is replaced by a stronger, concrete publication result showing the technique reproduces HH model outputs, narrowing the claim and increasing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All three models achieve high precision, recall and accuracy on test sets derived from 13 datasets used during training and on an independent test dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models trained on RNA-seq data recognize transcriptome signatures that are consistent across different tumors.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more conceptual/result-oriented statement about model recognition of consistent signatures rather than detailed performance metrics, clarifying the emphasis from numeric performance to generalizability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genes that are commonly altered in cancer by expression or splicing variations are under strong evolutionary and selective constraints.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genes composing the cancer transcriptome signatures are not frequently affected by mutations or genomic alterations.
alignments.rationale: The preprint posits evolutionary/selection constraints, while the publication reports a related empirical observation (low mutation frequency); the framing shifts from evolutionary inference to descriptive observation, so the claim is revised with low matching confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genes composing our cancer transcriptome signatures are not frequently affected by mutations or genomic alterations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genes composing the cancer transcriptome signatures are not frequently affected by mutations or genomic alterations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result with essentially the same content; the publication lists it with slightly lower expressed certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deregulation of RNA-processing genes and aberrant splicing are pervasive features across a large array of solid tumor types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deregulation of RNA-processing genes and aberrant splicing are pervasive features across a large array of solid tumor types.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward into the publication without substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transcriptomic features that we highlight here define cancer signatures that may reflect causal variations or consequences of disease state, or a combination of both.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transcriptomic features that are commonly shared between cancer types can be identified using RNA-seq samples from various normal and tumor tissues.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes identification of shared transcriptomic features using RNA-seq, which clarifies the methodological/result focus of the preprint's more interpretive claim about causality vs consequence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nutrient composition of rewards strongly influenced monkeys’ choices and learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nutrient composition of rewards strongly influenced learning and choices in monkeys.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical result with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Monkeys preferred rewards high in nutrient content and showed individual preferences for specific nutrients such as sugar and fat.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Preferences for specific nutrients affected how monkeys adapted to changing reward probabilities.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preference result in terms of its behavioral consequence (adaptation to changing probabilities), clarifying the functional implication of the preprint finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Monkeys learned faster from preferred nutrient rewards and chose them frequently even when associated with lower reward probability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Preferences for specific nutrients affected how monkeys adapted to changing reward probabilities.
alignments.rationale: The publication captures the same behavioral pattern (choice/learning under varying probabilities) but emphasizes adaptation to changing probabilities rather than the specific phrasing about learning speed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A nutrient-sensitive reinforcement learning model captured the processes of how monkeys updated the value of individual sugar and fat components of expected rewards.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A nutrient-sensitive reinforcement learning model captured the processes of value updating based on nutrient components.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the model result more confidently (high certainty) compared with the preprint's medium certainty, but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating nutrient-value functions into reinforcement learning models may enhance their biological validity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating biologically critical nutrient rewards into the reinforcement learning framework enhances its ecological validity.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the suggestion with stronger ecological framing but retains the same conclusion about increased validity when nutrient-specific values are incorporated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: People can learn to estimate the velocity of a fluctuating environment to make predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model incorporating a velocity component demonstrates a higher predictive accuracy compared to the standard delta-rule and the Kalman Filter.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports model performance that operationalizes the preprint's conceptual claim about learning velocity; the emphasis shifts from human capability to model predictive advantage, clarifying the empirical support.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning rates for velocity and decision noise are influenced by uncertainty constraints represented by the signal-to-noise ratio.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learning rate of velocity and the decision noise change depending on the value of the signal-to-noise ratio.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this specific dependency directly and without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hierarchical model provides a better account of individual variability and can generalize predictions about new subjects in different experimental conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical Bayesian modeling framework allows for prior predictions in untested signal-to-noise conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the framework's ability to generate prior predictions for untested conditions, which clarifies and exemplifies the preprint's broader claim about generalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generic recurrent neural networks used predominantly in the field generalize poorly on optogenetic perturbations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generic recurrent neural networks used predominantly in the field generalize poorly on optogenetic perturbations.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this empirical result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some biological inductive biases improve the model prediction on perturbed trials in both a simulated dataset and a dataset recorded in mice in vivo.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some biological inductive biases improve the model prediction on perturbed trials in both a simulated dataset and a dataset recorded in mice in vivo.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward this result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradients of the fitted recurrent neural network can be used to target micro-perturbations in the recorded circuits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradients of the fitted recurrent neural network can be used to target micro-perturbations in the recorded circuits.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same methodological/analytical claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model has potential utility to bias an animal's behavior and study cortical circuit mechanisms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The alternative recurrent neural network model incorporates biologically informed inductive biases like structured connectivity of excitatory and inhibitory neurons and spiking neuron dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The preprint cautiously suggested potential utility; the publication instead emphasizes concrete architectural contributions and presents them with higher certainty, broadening and shifting the focus of the original tentative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study achieved a 59.6% accuracy for correctly predicting lysine glycation using one physical property for characterization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the isoelectric point as the most promising property for characterization, the model achieved a 59.6% accuracy for predicting lysine glycation.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives the same numeric result but specifies the single property (isoelectric point) used, so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining two properties, mass and torsion angle, increased the accuracy of predicting lysine glycation to 59.9%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining the properties mass and torsion angle increased the accuracy of predicting lysine glycation to approximately 60%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same combination and essentially the same accuracy (rounded), so the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach developed in this study can aid in narrowing down possible sites of lysine glycation in protein targets for further analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach contributes to the understanding of glycation principles and aids in narrowing down possible sites of lysine glycation in protein targets.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim about aiding site prioritization but additionally frames the approach as contributing to broader understanding, thereby broadening the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-stressed pathological gamblers displayed lower model-based learning than healthy controls.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Non-stressed problem gamblers displayed lower model-based learning than healthy controls.
alignments.rationale: Wording differs only in terminology (pathological/problem gamblers) but the result is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stress-induced cortisol response has a deleterious effect on the orchestration between model-based and model-free learning in healthy controls but not in pathological gamblers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stress-induced cortisol response has a deleterious effect on the orchestration between model-based and model-free learning in healthy controls but not in problem gamblers.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same differential effect, with only terminology change; content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neither working memory nor IQ mediated the effects of stress on the orchestration between model-based and model-free learning.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about mediation by working memory or IQ appears in the publication, so the preprint claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pathological gamblers exhibit an abnormal pattern of modulation of stress on the orchestration between model-based and model-free learning despite a normal cortisol response to stress.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An abnormal pattern of modulation of stress on the orchestration between model-based and model-free learning was found among problem gamblers.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core abnormal-modulation conclusion but drops the explicit qualifier about a normal cortisol response, so the claim is revised and somewhat tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel Nucleotide-Level Deep Neural Network (NLDNN) improves TF binding prediction within and across species.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel Nucleotide-Level Deep Neural Network (NLDNN) proposed in this study outperforms competing methods in predicting TF binding within and across species.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the improvement claim more explicit by stating it outperforms competing methods, increasing the strength/precision of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NLDNN treats TF binding prediction as a nucleotide-level regression task.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly restates the regression framing at nucleotide level among the reported claims, so this methodological wording appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dual-path framework for adversarial training enhances cross-species prediction performance by aligning the domain space of human and mouse species.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adversarial training of NLDNN improves cross-species prediction performance between humans and mice.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core result that adversarial training improves cross-species performance but omits some mechanistic phrasing (dual-path/domain alignment); the claim is presented more strongly as a result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cross-species predictive performance is consistently worse than within-species performance due to rapid evolutionary turnover of TF binding sites.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the specific explanation (evolutionary turnover) or explicit comparative statement as phrased, so this preprint claim was not preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ANN models achieve a median prediction error of 1.39%, which is 10-fold smaller than for previously published models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The constructed ANN models achieve a median prediction error of 1.59% on an independent test set.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a higher median error (1.59% vs 1.39%) on an independent test set and does not replicate the 10-fold comparison exactly, so the numerical claim is revised and slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The constructed linear and neural network models predict the distribution of glycans on N-glycosylation sites.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes ANN models in the reported claims and does not assert equal prominence for linear models, so the original broader method claim is narrowed/revised in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models are trained on data containing normalized B4GALT levels in Chinese Hamster Ovary cells.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim in the provided set repeats this specific training-data detail, so this preprint detail appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article discusses issues with other models reported in the literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work's model has a narrow error distribution compared to other models in the literature.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the comparison concretely (narrow error distribution vs others), which clarifies and operationalizes the preprint's more general discussion of issues with other models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All of the software used in this work is provided, allowing other researchers to reproduce the work and reuse or improve the code.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The software used in this work is openly provided for other researchers to reproduce and improve upon.
alignments.rationale: Both formulations state that the software is openly provided for reproducibility; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BOLD Moments Dataset (BMD) is a repository of whole-brain fMRI responses to over 1,000 short naturalistic video clips of visual events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The BOLD Moments Dataset (BMD) is a repository of whole-brain fMRI responses to over 1000 short naturalistic video clips of visual events.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the dataset contribution with essentially identical wording and numbers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The brain represents word- and sentence-level descriptions of visual events and identifies correlates of video memorability scores extending into the parietal cortex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The brain represents word- and sentence-level descriptions of visual events and identifies correlates of video memorability scores extending into the parietal cortex.
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a match in hierarchical processing between cortical regions of interest and video-computable deep neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a match in hierarchical processing between cortical regions of interest and video-computable deep neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BMD successfully captures temporal dynamics of visual events at second resolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BMD successfully captures temporal dynamics of visual events at second resolution.
alignments.rationale: The claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BMD offers new perspectives and accelerates research on the human brain basis of visual event perception.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BMD offers new perspectives and accelerates research on the human brain basis of visual event perception.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about the dataset's contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A bioinspired spiking neural network model of the sensorimotor peripheral whisker system was developed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed bioinspired spiking neural network model accurately represents the sensorimotor peripheral whisker system.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the development claim but adds that the model "accurately represents" the system, making the claim more specific and stronger.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network was embedded in a virtual mouse robot using the Neurorobotics Platform.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The virtual mouse robot successfully integrates the peripheral whisker system with an adaptive cerebellar network controller.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the embedding/integration idea but shifts emphasis to successful integration with an adaptive cerebellar controller, adding new technical detail beyond the preprint description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The whole system was able to drive active whisking with learning capability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system demonstrates the capability to drive active whisking with learning ability, reflecting neural correlates of behavior observed in mice.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the system's active whisking and learning capability and adds that this reflects neural correlates observed in mice, broadening the claim's connection to experimental data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system's performance matched neural correlates of behaviour experimentally recorded in mice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system demonstrates the capability to drive active whisking with learning ability, reflecting neural correlates of behavior observed in mice.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the preprint's neural-correlate correspondence with the performance/behavioral demonstration, keeping the substantive content but packaging it with the active-whisking claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepCor outperforms CompCor on a variety of simulated datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepCor outperforms CompCor by 215%.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the outperforming-CompCor claim but quantifies it precisely, thereby clarifying and strengthening the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepCor enhances differences in connectivity between brain networks in real datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report an enhancement of connectivity differences; instead it reports enhancement of BOLD responses to face stimuli, so the preprint connectivity-specific claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improving denoising methods could lead to novel discoveries in neuroscience research.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the broader speculative conclusion about enabling novel discoveries, so the preprint's general concluding claim is not present.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepCor outperforms other state-of-the-art denoising approaches on a variety of simulated datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a broader claim comparing DeepCor to multiple state-of-the-art methods that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepCor enhances BOLD signal responses to face stimuli in real fMRI data.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a specific real-data finding (enhanced BOLD responses to face stimuli) that does not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed nuclei segmentation and classification tool can facilitate pathologists in analyzing the tumor microenvironment from H&E pathology images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed deep learning-based computation model can segment various cell types from standard pathology images.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the segmentation contribution, specifying it as a deep learning-based model and emphasizing segmentation of various cell types, consistent with the preprint's tool claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prognostic model developed using the extracted cell spatial organization-related features showed that the predicted high-risk group had significantly worse survival than the low-risk group.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predicted high-risk group in the prognostic model showed significantly worse survival than the low-risk group (P = 0.001), with a HR of 2.23 (1.37-3.65) after adjusting for clinical variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides statistical details (P-value, hazard ratio, adjustment) that clarify and strengthen the preprint's prognostic-survival result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The image-derived tumor microenvironment features were significantly correlated with the gene expression of biological pathways.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Image-derived TME features significantly correlated with the gene expression of biological pathways.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about significant correlations between image-derived features and pathway gene expression.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spatial organization of different cell types is predictive of patient survival.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spatial organization of different cell types in tumor tissues is predictive of patient survival.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint conclusion that cell-type spatial organization predicts survival without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model developed from lung adenocarcinoma pathology images can be adapted for other types of cancers.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention or demonstrate adaptation of the lung adenocarcinoma model to other cancer types, so this adaptation claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The non-linear Drift-Diffusion Model (nl-DDM) performs better than the traditional drift-diffusion model for an equivalent complexity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The non-linear Drift-Diffusion Model (nl-DDM) performs better than the traditional drift-diffusion model for an equivalent complexity.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this performance comparison identically to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nl-DDM captures time effects better than the traditional drift-diffusion model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nl-DDM captures time effects better than the traditional DDM.
alignments.rationale: The publication's formulation is equivalent to the preprint's claim about capturing time effects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nl-DDM allows for the existence of several trajectories to the decision boundary, addressing limitations of the traditional model.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state the multiple-trajectory conceptualization as a separate claim, so this specific formulation from the preprint is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nl-DDM provides a better analysis of across-trial variability for perceptual decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nl-DDM provides a better analysis of across-trial variability for perceptual decisions compared to the DDM.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about improved analysis of across-trial variability, framed as a comparison to the DDM.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The nl-DDM accounts for peri-stimulus influences more effectively than the DDM.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an additional claim about peri-stimulus influences that does not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is feasible to derive parsimonious spatially explicit population models using spatialised population measurements and geographic habitat data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is feasible to derive parsimonious spatially explicit population models using spatialised population measurements and geographic habitat data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this feasibility conclusion directly from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biological measurements represent a significant bottleneck against the widespread usage of reaction-diffusion models in conservation biology and agricultural plant health.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high cost of biological measurements is a significant bottleneck to the widespread usage of reaction-diffusion models.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the bottleneck as the high cost of measurements, clarifying the preprint's more general bottleneck statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classical methods for optimal experimental design can be useful for spatial ecologists to minimize variance of parameter estimates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classical methods for optimal experimental design can be useful for spatial ecologists to minimize variance of parameter estimates.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution regarding the utility of classical optimal design methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper demonstrates how to construct a priori experimental designs that enable optimal experimental setups with pre and post hoc filtering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents two case studies that demonstrate how to construct a priori experimental designs under constraints.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the general demonstration to two concrete case studies and frames the designs as developed under constraints, modifying the preprint's broader claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Reaction-diffusion models are commonly used in conservation biology and agricultural plant health for applications such as landscape planning and epidemiological surveillance.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an added contextual claim about common applications of reaction-diffusion models that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional virtual screening methods are often inaccurate, with most compounds nominated in a virtual screen not engaging the intended target protein to any detectable extent. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional virtual screening methods are often inaccurate, with most compounds nominated in a virtual screen not engaging the intended target protein to any detectable extent. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same assertion verbatim; there is no change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Emerging machine learning approaches have made significant progress in improving hit-finding discovery rates. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Emerging machine learning approaches have made significant progress in improving hit-finding discovery rates. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: vScreenML 2.0 outperforms other widely-used tools for virtual screening hit discovery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: vScreenML 2.0 outperforms other widely used tools for virtual screening hit discovery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about vScreenML 2.0's superior performance is carried into the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a machine learning-guided platform that integrates cell-free DNA assembly, cell-free gene expression, and functional assays to rapidly map fitness landscapes across protein sequence space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning-guided platform was developed to rapidly map fitness landscapes across protein sequence space and optimize enzymes for multiple chemical reactions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the development and mapping claim but explicitly adds optimization for chemical reactions, broadening the stated application of the platform.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We applied this platform to engineer amide synthetases by evaluating substrate preference for 1,217 enzyme variants in 10,953 unique reactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform was applied to engineer amide synthetases by evaluating substrate preference for 1217 enzyme variants in 10,953 unique reactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numbers and experimental application are identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We used these data to build augmented ridge regression ML models for predicting amide synthetase variants capable of making 9 small molecule pharmaceuticals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ML-predicted enzyme variants demonstrate 1.6- to 42-fold improved activity relative to the parent for nine small molecule pharmaceuticals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from describing predictive model construction to reporting concrete validated improvements (fold-changes), revising the claim toward stronger experimental outcomes and increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our ML-guided, cell-free framework promises to accelerate enzyme engineering by enabling iterative exploration of protein sequence space to build specialized biocatalysts in parallel. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML-guided, cell-free framework promises to accelerate enzyme engineering by enabling iterative exploration of protein sequence space. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core promise but omits the explicit phrasing about building specialized biocatalysts in parallel, narrowing the scope of the stated outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A rule-based machine learning workflow was developed to uncover quantifiably valid and generalizable diagnostic fragments for O-glycans. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A rule-based machine learning workflow was developed to uncover quantifiably valid and generalizable diagnostic fragments for O-glycomics spectra. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the context as O-glycomics spectra, making the contribution more explicit and slightly narrower in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fragmentation rules established by the workflow can robustly distinguish common O-glycan isomers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fragmentation rules established by the workflow can robustly distinguish common O-glycan isomers for reduced glycans in negative ion mode. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds methodological constraints (reduced glycans, negative ion mode), narrowing where the rules are validated while preserving the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resource created is expected to improve glycan annotation accuracy and make annotations more transparent and homogeneous across analysts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resource created aims to improve glycan annotation accuracy and make annotations more transparent and homogeneous across analysts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the intended impact of the resource with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Molecular datasets combined with ecological surveys can precisely delimit the nature of cave endemism and identify conservation priorities for microendemic species. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Molecular datasets combined with ecological surveys can precisely delimit the nature of cave endemism and identify conservation priorities for microendemic species. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sequencing of ultraconserved elements of Tegenaria revealed clear species limits and the incidence of previously unidentified, potential cryptic species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analyses identified clear and well-supported genetic breaks that closely align with morphologically diagnosable units. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same finding about clear species limits (genetic breaks aligning with diagnosable units), matching the preprint's core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The R package canaper and Categorical Analysis of Neo- and Paleo-Endemism (CANAPE) were employed to generate conservation metrics that are informative for future policy. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conservation assessments determined that five of the seven troglobitic Israeli species of Tegenaria are critically endangered. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a concrete conservation outcome derived from assessments (specific species classified as critically endangered), which revises the preprint's methodological/contribution claim into a specific applied result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: APOGEE 2 improves the accuracy of pathogenicity predictions for interpreting missense mitochondrial variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: APOGEE 2 is an ensemble method designed to improve the accuracy of pathogenicity predictions for interpreting missense mitochondrial variants. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames APOGEE 2 as a method designed to improve predictions rather than definitively stating improvement, which softens the assertive result into a design/contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: APOGEE 2 features an improved machine learning method and a curated training set for enhanced performance metrics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: APOGEE 2 features an improved machine learning method and a curated training set for enhanced performance metrics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution about methodological improvements and curation is carried forward verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: APOGEE 2 offers region-wise assessments of genome fragility and mechanistic analyses of specific amino acids. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: APOGEE 2 offers region-wise assessments of genome fragility and mechanistic analyses of specific amino acids that cause perceptible long-range effects on protein structure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the region-wise and mechanistic analysis claim but adds specificity about the types of amino-acid effects characterized, clarifying the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: APOGEE 2's ability to address challenges in interpreting mitochondrial missense variants makes it an essential tool in mitochondrial genetics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: APOGEE 2’s ability to address challenges in interpreting mitochondrial missense variants makes it an essential tool in the field of mitochondrial genetics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the strong concluding claim about APOGEE 2's utility in the field with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The one-class logistic regression machine learning algorithm can extract transcriptomic or proteomic features from non-transformed stem cells to identify dedifferentiated cell states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The one-class logistic regression machine learning algorithm can extract transcriptomic features from non-transformed stem cells to identify dedifferentiated cell states in tumors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same algorithmic capability but omits proteomic features (preprint included proteomic), so the claim is revised and its scope narrowed while confidence remains high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This method identifies single cell states in metastatic tumor cell populations without the requirement of cell annotation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method identifies single cell states in metastatic tumor cell populations without the requirement of cell annotation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same emphasis and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stemness indices derived from this method are applicable across a variety of spatial transcriptomic and proteomic technologies for identifying oncogenic cell types in the tumor microenvironment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the application of a machine learning tool across five emerging spatial transcriptomic and proteomic technologies to identify oncogenic stem-like cell types in the tumor microenvironment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more specific enumeration (five technologies) and raises certainty; the core applicability claim is preserved but clarified and narrowed to specific technologies with higher confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new computational screening strategy effectively classifies bioactive compounds and plants using a word embedding algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifier showed good performance in binary classification for both compounds and plant genera. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the classifier's effective performance for compounds and plants, matching the preprint result despite omitting the algorithmic detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifier demonstrated good performance in binary classification for both compounds and plant genera. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifier showed good performance in binary classification for both compounds and plant genera. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This claim is effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Essential oils from Lindera triloba and Cinnamomum sieboldii exhibit antimicrobial activity against Staphylococcus aureus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The strategy led to the discovery of antimicrobial activity of essential oils from Lindera triloba and Cinnamomum sieboldii against Staphylococcus aureus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same discovery and maintains the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine-learning classification in semantic space can be a highly efficient approach for exploring bioactive plant extracts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine-learning classification in semantic space can be a highly efficient approach for exploring bioactive plant extracts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion but presents it with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning has the ability to find novel relationships between biological features. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can find novel relationships between biological features. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We constructed a dataset of 11,801 gene features for 31,522 Arabidopsis thaliana genes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We constructed a dataset of 11,801 gene features for 31,522 Arabidopsis thaliana genes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The detected linked features are visualised as a Feature Important Network (FIN), which can be mined to reveal a variety of novel biological insights pertaining to gene function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The detected linked features are visualised as a Feature Important Network (FIN), which can reveal novel biological insights pertaining to gene function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the result and implications of the FIN visualization with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate how FIN can be used to generate novel insights into gene function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FIN can be used to generate novel insights into gene function. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the demonstration as a general capability of FIN; the core claim that FIN yields novel insights is preserved and clarified as a broader capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present the FINder database to make this network easily accessible to the scientific community. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present the FINder database to make the network easily accessible to the scientific community. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution of releasing the FINder database is maintained identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep generative model can replace control samples in differential gene expression analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generative model enables control-free, single-sample differential expression analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint claim more explicitly as control-free, single-sample DE analysis, preserving the core contribution but clarifying the formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model accurately identifies representations close to the tissue of origin when applied to cancer samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific claim about identifying representations closest to tissue of origin; this specific performance claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach finds subtype-specific cancer driver and marker genes with high specificity and outperforms DESeq2 in detecting differentially expressed genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In breast cancer, the approach selects marker genes and outperforms a state-of-the-art method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication demonstrates outperforming a state-of-the-art method specifically in breast cancer, narrowing the preprint's broader claim about subtype-specific performance across cancers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The significant genes found using the model are highly enriched within cancer-specific driver genes across different cancer types. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant genes identified by the model are enriched in driver genes across cancers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the enrichment finding across cancers with similar wording and medium certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The in silico closest normal provides a more favorable comparison than control samples. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The in silico closest normal provides a more favorable comparison than control samples. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conclusion but presents it with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subject behaviour deviates qualitatively from Bayes-optimal, showing an unexpected positive effect of N (the number of visual items) on the false-alarm rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subject behaviour deviates qualitatively from Bayes-optimal, showing an unexpected positive effect of N on the false-alarm rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical result verbatim with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several 'point-estimating' observer models fit subject behaviour better than the Bayesian model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several 'point-estimating' observer models fit subject behaviour better than the Bayesian model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this model-comparison result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The brain may implement partially committal variants of Bayesian models when detecting latent causes based on complex real world data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The brain may implement partially committal variants of Bayesian models when detecting latent causes based on complex real world data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion about brain implementation is preserved identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepBGC offers more accurate BGC identification and an improved ability to extrapolate and identify novel BGC classes compared to existing tools.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepBGC offers reduced false positive rates in BGC identification compared to existing machine-learning tools.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the accuracy claim in terms of reduced false positives, which clarifies and narrows the general "more accurate" statement from the preprint while maintaining similar confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Application of DeepBGC to bacterial genomes uncovered previously undetectable BGCs that may code for natural products with novel biologic activities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Application of DeepBGC to bacterial genomes uncovered previously undetectable putative BGCs that may code for natural products with novel biologic activities.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with only minor wording change (addition of "putative"); the meaning and scope remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The improved accuracy and classification ability of DeepBGC represents a significant step forward for in-silico BGC identification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The improved accuracy and classification ability of DeepBGC represents a major addition to in-silico BGC identification.
alignments.rationale: The publication uses stronger language ("major addition") to describe the contribution, which clarifies and amplifies the claimed impact relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FAVITES is a robust framework for simulating realistic datasets for epidemics caused by fast-evolving pathogens like HIV.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FAVITES is a robust framework for simulating realistic datasets for epidemics caused by fast-evolving pathogens like HIV.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FAVITES creates a generative model to produce contact networks, transmission networks, phylogenetic trees, and sequence datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FAVITES creates a generative model to produce contact networks, transmission networks, phylogenetic trees, and sequence datasets.
alignments.rationale: Identical description of the framework's generative capabilities in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulated data from FAVITES can be used to study the impact of increased treatment efforts on epidemiological outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulated data from FAVITES can be used to study the impact of increased treatment efforts on epidemiological outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two transmission network reconstruction methods were studied for their effectiveness in detecting fast-growing clusters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two transmission network reconstruction methods were studied for their effectiveness in detecting fast-growing clusters.
alignments.rationale: The study of two methods and their effectiveness is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UNMIX-ME is a deep learning-based fluorescence unmixing routine that enables quantitative fluorophore unmixing by simultaneously using both spectral and temporal signatures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: UNMIX-ME is a deep learning-based fluorescence unmixing routine that enables quantitative fluorophore unmixing by simultaneously using both spectral and temporal signatures.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution is restated exactly in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UNMIX-ME was trained and validated using an in silico framework that replicates the data acquisition process of a compressive hyperspectral fluorescent lifetime imaging platform.
alignments.pub_claim_text: UNMIX-ME was trained and validated using an in silico framework that replicates the data acquisition process of a compressive hyperspectral fluorescent lifetime imaging platform.
alignments.rationale: Training and validation approach is described the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UNMIX-ME was benchmarked against a conventional LSQ method for both tri and quadri-exponential simulated samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: UNMIX-ME was benchmarked against a conventional LSQ method for tri and quadri-exponential simulated samples.
alignments.rationale: Benchmarking against the LSQ method for tri/quadri-exponential samples is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The potential of UNMIX-ME was assessed for NIR FRET in vitro and in vivo for small animal experimental data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The potential of UNMIX-ME was assessed for NIR FRET in vitro and in vivo preclinical applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes "small animal experimental data" to broader "preclinical applications," which broadens the scope while keeping the core assessment claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest-based model developed using SVC-L1 selected features achieved a maximum performance AUC of 0.80 with MCC 0.61 on the validation data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest-based model developed using SVC-L1 selected features achieved a maximum performance AUC of 0.80 with MCC 0.61 on validation data.
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics and description are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An ensemble-based method combining the best performing machine learning model with motifs achieved an AUC of 0.92 with MCC 0.83 on the independent validation dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An ensemble-based method combining the best performing machine learning model with exclusive motifs achieved an AUC of 0.92 with MCC 0.83 on the independent validation dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies "exclusive motifs," which tightens the description of the motifs used (narrowing the claim) but preserves the reported performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A web server and standalone package ThPPred was developed to facilitate the scientific community in predicting and designing druggable proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A web server and standalone package called 'ThPPred' has been proposed to facilitate the prediction and designing of druggable proteins.
alignments.rationale: The preprint reports development (strong contribution) while the publication frames the software as proposed with reduced certainty; this revises and tempers the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified 51 characteristic genes of respiratory diseases and 10 potential genes exacerbating HF, primarily involved in innate immune response, inflammation, and coagulation pathways.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified 51 common molecules among influenza, COVID-19, and community-acquired pneumonia that impact heart failure.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the finding of 51 common entities affecting heart failure but describes them as "molecules" and emphasizes impact on HF; this clarifies wording without changing the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms identified RSAD2 and IFI44L as key genes with high accuracy (AUC > 0.7).
alignments.pub_claim_text: RSAD2 and IFI44L were identified as key genes related to heart failure progression using machine learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates identification of these two genes by machine learning; the associated accuracy is reported elsewhere (ROC verification), so the core claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ssGSEA indicated RSAD2’s involvement in complement and coagulation cascades, while IFI44L is associated with myocardial contraction in HF progression.
alignments.rationale: The specific pathway-level ssGSEA associations reported in the preprint are not present among the publication's summarized claims, indicating removal of this detailed assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Immune infiltration analysis revealed significant differences in eight immune cell types between HF patients and healthy controls.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not report immune infiltration findings or the specific eight cell-type differences, so this preprint result appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings enhance the understanding of molecular interactions between respiratory diseases and heart failure, paving the way for future research and therapeutic strategies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Six drugs, including acetohexamide, may have potential therapeutic effects on heart failure aggravated by respiratory diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication converts the general forward-looking conclusion into concrete therapeutic hypotheses (specific drugs), which clarifies and amplifies the practical implications of the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning model MS2Lipid achieved an accuracy of 95.5% in predicting lipid subclasses from MS/MS queries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MS2Lipid model achieved an accuracy of 97.4% in classifying lipid subclasses from MS/MS queries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims report the model's accuracy for the same task but the published paper reports a different (higher) numeric accuracy, so the claim is a revised numeric result with increased reported performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MS2Lipid outperformed the CANOPUS ontology prediction program, providing correct annotations for 38.7% of the same test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention CANOPUS comparison or the 38.7% figure, so this specific comparative claim appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average accuracy of MS2Lipid exceeded 87.4% when validated using various datasets from different machines and curators. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average accuracy of MS2Lipid exceeded 87.2% when validated with various datasets from different instruments and curators. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim corresponds to the same validation result with a very small numeric adjustment and slightly clarified wording (instruments vs machines), so the claim is retained with minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The function of MS2Lipid was showcased by the annotation of novel esterified bile acids, which were significantly increased in obese patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MS2Lipid demonstrated the utility of annotating microbiota-derived esterified bile acids, which were significantly increased in fecal samples of obese patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same finding but specifies microbiota-derived and fecal samples, adding detail and narrowing the context of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning model provides an independent criterion for lipid subclass classification and an environment for annotating previously unknown lipid metabolites. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning model provides an independent criterion for lipid subclass classification, enhancing the annotation of lipid metabolites. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core conclusion about providing an independent classification criterion and expands wording toward enhanced annotation, a clarification of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms can predict peptides that are effective against Hepatitis C. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the application and fine-tuning of machine learning algorithms to predict peptides that are effective against Hepatitis C virus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same general result while adding mention of application and fine-tuning, which clarifies the original claim without changing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest algorithm produced the best performance with an accuracy of about 90% in predicting anti-HepC peptides. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest algorithm produced the best performance with an accuracy of about 92% in predicting anti-hepatitis C peptides. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims identify random forest as best, but the published accuracy differs (higher), so the numeric result was revised and reported with slightly amplified performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The distribution of hydrophobicity and polarizability, as well as the frequencies of specific di-peptide motifs, are key predictors for identifying anti-HepC peptides. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Key predictors for identifying anti-hepatitis C peptides include the distributions of hydrophobicity, polarizability, coil-forming residues, frequency of glycine residues, and the existence of dipeptide motifs VL, LV, and CC. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the originally mentioned predictors and several additional specific features, expanding and clarifying the set of important predictors (broadened scope).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model can be accessed through the Pred-AHCP web server, facilitating the prediction and re-engineering of anti-HepC peptides. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed model can be accessed through the Pred-AHCP web server, facilitating the prediction and re-engineering of anti-hepatitis C peptides for designing peptide-based therapeutics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the web-accessible model claim but extends its framed utility toward designing peptide-based therapeutics, broadening the claimed applications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study proposes exploring similar strategies for designing peptide inhibitors effective against other viruses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not explicitly propose extending the strategy to other viruses; that forward-looking suggestion from the preprint is not present in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed ML model can be used for validating peptide sequences generated using generative artificial intelligence methods for further optimization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This publication-level suggestion about using the model to validate generative-AI-produced sequences is not present in the preprint and thus represents an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Basic artifact rejection improved model performance in age and sex prediction analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a machine learning method that uses protein three-dimensional structures from AlphaFold to predict how a variant will influence changes to a gene's downstream biological pathways. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
