alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSCAb outperforms similar methods lacking side chain context in discriminating near-native structures from sets of decoys.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSCAb outperforms similar methods lacking side-chain context in discriminating near-native structures from sets of decoys.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSCAb predicts side chain conformations competitively when compared to alternative rotamer repacking methods that require an input backbone structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSCAb predicts side-chain conformations competitively when compared to alternative rotamer repacking methods that require an input backbone structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same comparative performance claim with matching scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSCAb improves antibody structure prediction with accurate side chain modeling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSCAb improves antibody structure prediction with accurate side-chain modeling.
alignments.rationale: This claim appears verbatim in the publication; it matches exactly the preprint conclusion about improved antibody structure prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSCAb is adaptable to applications in docking of antibody-antigen complexes and design of new therapeutic antibody sequences.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or rephrase this application/design-oriented claim, so it appears to have been omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The network requires only sequence as input, making it particularly useful for antibodies without any known backbone conformations.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a new claim about sequence-only input that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep-LUMEN is an object detection algorithm that can automatically uncover subtle differences in epithelial spheroid morphology from brightfield images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep-LUMEN is an object detection algorithm that can automatically uncover subtle differences in epithelial spheroid morphology from brightfield images.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Deep-LUMEN assay can track changes in the luminal structure of tissue spheroids and distinguish between polarized and non-polarized lung epithelial spheroids.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning-based model robustly differentiates survival subpopulations of hepatocellular carcinoma patients in six cohorts.

alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4 consistently outperformed the other models, demonstrating a high capacity to interpret textual data and extract relevant information, with the percentage of correct outputs often exceeding 90%.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature analysis for iSEE underlines the significance of evolutionary conservations for quantitative prediction of mutation effects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Escitalopram and mifepristone inhibit hypertrophy of cultured cardiomyocytes in two contexts.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ARCTICA effectively simulates global-scale metabolic flux control.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert ARCTICA's effectiveness at simulating global-scale metabolic flux control; the publication omits the species in the short claim but meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Photosynthetic bioproduction is mainly governed by enzymes from the Calvin–Benson–Bassham (CBB) cycle, rather than by those involved in the biosynthesis of the end-product.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The photosynthetic bioproduction is mainly governed by enzymes within the Calvin-Benson-Bassham (CBB) cycle, rather than by those involved in the biosynthesis of the end-product.
alignments.rationale: This is the same result restated with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The catalytic capacity of the CBB cycle limits the photosynthetic activity and downstream pathways.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The catalytic capacity of the CBB cycle limits the photosynthetic activity and downstream pathways.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in both versions about CBB catalytic capacity limiting photosynthesis and downstream pathways.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase (RuBisCO) is a major, but not the most, limiting step in the CBB cycle.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase (RuBisCO) is a major, but not the most, limiting step within the CBB cycle.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same nuanced claim about RuBisCO's role as a significant but not the top limiting step.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ARCTICA serves as a valuable pipeline for understanding cellular physiology and predicting rate-limiting steps in genome-scale metabolic networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ARCTICA provides guidance for bioengineering of cyanobacteria.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the tool's utility toward guidance for cyanobacterial bioengineering rather than the broader pipeline claim, narrowing scope and presenting a somewhat more modest claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An individually tailored machine learning approach can identify person-specific patterns of neural activity during mental rotation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An individually tailored machine learning approach can identify person-specific patterns of neural activity during mental rotation.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is retained verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The regression model predicted the reaction times significantly more accurately than when only the average reaction time was used for prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The regression model predicted the reaction times significantly more accurately when using person-specific EEG activity compared to using the average reaction time.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same empirical result that person-specific information yields more accurate RT predictions than using the average.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictions based on another person’s data were significantly less accurate compared to within-person predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictions made on another person's data were significantly less accurate compared to within-person predictions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this comparative result exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Considering person-specific reaction times and topographical activity patterns substantially improved a model’s generalizability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Considering person-specific reaction times and topographical activity patterns substantially improved a model's generalizability.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about improved generalizability with person-specific features is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A more individualized approach towards neural correlates can improve their predictive performance of behavioural responses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Considering person-specific reaction times and topographical activity patterns substantially improved a model's generalizability.
alignments.rationale: This preprint conclusion is effectively restated in the publication's claim about person-specific features improving model generalizability; the mapping is slightly less direct but consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TaxaHFE algorithm achieves an average 90% reduction in the number of features compared to using the most complete taxonomy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TaxaHFE algorithm achieves an average 90% reduction in the number of features compared to using the most complete taxonomy.
alignments.rationale: The result about feature reduction is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models based on TaxaHFE features achieved an average increase of 3.47% in receiver operator curve area under the curve (ROC-AUC) compared to the most resolved taxonomic level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models based on TaxaHFE features achieved an average increase of 3.47% in receiver operator curve area under the curve compared to the most resolved taxonomic level.
alignments.rationale: The performance improvement claim is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TaxaHFE introduces the novel ability to consider both categorical and continuous response variables to inform the feature set collapse.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TaxaHFE introduces the novel ability to consider both categorical and continuous response variables to inform the feature set collapse.
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TaxaHFE’s ability to reduce hierarchically organized features to a more information-rich subset increases the interpretability of models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TaxaHFE's ability to reduce hierarchically organized features to a more information-rich subset increases the interpretability of models.
alignments.rationale: The interpretability benefit is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A total of 95.8% of isolates were correctly identified at subspecies level using a Random Forest model with several confidence criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 96.5% of M. abscessus isolates were correctly identified at the subspecies level using a random forest model with specific confidence criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with a slightly different numeric value and more specific wording about the model's criteria, so the claim is clarified rather than substantively changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differences in geographic origin of the strains most affected the mass spectra of isolates. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differences in geographic origin of the strains had an impact on the protein spectra used for identification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed geographic origin as the major factor ('most affected'), while the publication tones this down to having an impact on spectra; the claim is clarified and narrowed with reduced strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two novel peaks with potential for subspecies differentiation were found after studying all protein peaks previously reported for this complex. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two novel peaks with potential for subspecies differentiation were found after studying all protein peaks previously reported for M. abscessus. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding about two novel peaks with equivalent scope and confidence, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methodology has proven to be a promising approach for rapid and accurate identification of subspecies of the M. abscessus complex using MALDI-TOF MS. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides results and specific findings but does not explicitly restate this broad concluding claim about ML methodology being a promising general approach, so the explicit conclusion is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supervised machine learning models can achieve the first quantitative Ψ occupancy measurement in human mRNAs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We applied our models to quantitatively profile ψ occupancy in five mRNA sites in datasets from seven human cell lines, demonstrating conserved and variable sites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication demonstrates quantitative profiling but limits it to five mRNA sites across several cell lines, which narrows the preprint's broader claim of general quantitative measurement in human mRNAs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different signal parameters are required to maximize Ψ classification accuracy for every site studied. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models demonstrate that the ionic current signal features important for accurate ψ classification are sequence dependent and encompass information extending beyond n + 2 and n − 2 nucleotides from the ψ site. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that signal features are sequence-dependent and extend beyond local k-mer windows, which aligns with and specifies the preprint's claim about site-specific signal parameters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying the machine learning model is critical for quantification, especially in low-abundance mRNAs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly assert that applying the model is 'critical' for quantification or emphasize low-abundance mRNAs specifically, so this stronger practical claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The engine developed can profile Ψ-occupancy across cell types and cell states. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We applied our models to quantitatively profile ψ occupancy in five mRNA sites in datasets from seven human cell lines, demonstrating conserved and variable sites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication demonstrates cross-cell-line profiling for specific sites, which supports the preprint claim but narrows it to demonstrated cases (five sites, seven lines) rather than a broad capability across all cell types/states.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PDAUG package provides a collection of tools, workflows, and datasets for rapid in-silico peptide library analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PDAUG package provides an integrated GUI-based toolset for peptide library analysis that does not require programming expertise. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the integrated GUI and no-programming requirement, which clarifies and specifies the preprint's description of the package without changing its core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PDAUG offers a GUI based toolset that allows researchers to build and distribute reproducible pipelines and workflows without programming expertise. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PDAUG package provides an integrated GUI-based toolset for peptide library analysis that does not require programming expertise. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability (GUI-based, no programming required for workflows/pipelines), so this claim is essentially unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usability of PDAUG is demonstrated on predicting anticancer properties of peptides using four different feature sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PDAUG demonstrates usability in predicting anticancer properties of peptides using four different feature sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same demonstration of usability with the same experimental setup, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PDAUG facilitates the combination with hundreds of compatible existing Galaxy tools for limitless analytic strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PDAUG toolset facilitates the combination with existing Galaxy tools for limitless analytic strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same claim about integration with Galaxy tools and broad analytic possibilities, matching the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed two-class machine-learning classification models to differentiate the early and late stages of invasive ductal carcinoma. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed two-class machine-learning classification models can differentiate between early and late stages of Invasive Ductal Carcinoma (IDC). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same development and capability of the two-class models, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction models are trained with RNA-seq gene expression profiles representing different IDC stages of 610 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction models are trained with RNA-seq gene expression profiles from 610 patients with different IDC stages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the training data and patient count identically, so this result claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a machine-learning classifier trained on datasets corresponding to IDC driver genes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly mention a classifier trained on IDC driver-gene-specific datasets, so this specific claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis conducted enables deeper insights into the stage-dependent molecular events accompanying breast ductal carcinoma progression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis provides deeper insights into the stage-dependent molecular events accompanying IDC progression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion about providing deeper molecular insights, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The server Duct-BRCA-CSP is publicly available to predict early stage from late stages of IDC based on input RNA-seq gene expression profiles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A web-server Duct-BRCA-CSP has been developed to predict early stage from late stages of IDC based on RNA-seq gene expression profiles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the web-server's development and purpose, matching the preprint claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SVM-based image classification approach achieved more than 95% accuracy for both recently and chronically HCV-infected individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SVM-based image classification approach achieved more than 95% accuracy for both recently and chronically HCV-infected individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the exact performance claim for the SVM-based classifier, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clustering on the data collected from 33 epidemiologically curated outbreaks yielded more than 97% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clustering on data collected from 33 epidemiologically curated outbreaks yielded more than 97% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same clustering accuracy on the same outbreak dataset, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel preprocessing approach transforms irregular genomic data into normalized image data, allowing classification and comparison of heterogeneous populations as image classification problems. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed preprocessing approach transforms irregular genomic data into normalized image data, allowing for the classification and comparison of heterogeneous populations as image classification problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same preprocessing contribution (with slightly stronger certainty), so the claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Inversion Transformer-based Neural Ranking (ITNR) model reliably and consistently outperforms state-of-the-art Learning-to-rank models in predicting patient-specific drug responses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments show that ITNR reliably and consistently outperforms state-of-the-art LTR models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim restates the preprint result about ITNR outperforming SOTA with essentially the same wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ITNR utilizes a Context-Aware-Transformer architecture to model inter-item dependencies in drug response prediction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ITNR utilizes genomic features and a transformer architecture to predict patient-specific drug responses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe transformer-based architecture use; the publication frames it with genomic features and prediction outcome, which clarifies and broadens the description of inputs and aims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel loss function introduced in ITNR, based on Inversion and Approximate Permutation matrices, contributes to its improved performance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention the novel loss function or attribute performance gains to it, indicating this contribution was omitted from the highlighted published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational results indicate that ITNR leads to substantially improved performance compared to baseline methods across all performance metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments show that ITNR reliably and consistently outperforms state-of-the-art LTR models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's assertion of improvement "across all performance metrics" is softened in the publication to a general statement of reliable outperformance, narrowing the scope and tempering the original absolute phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning provides unprecedented precision in quantifying rodent motor behavior post-stroke. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can accurately quantify rodent motor behavior post-stroke. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down "unprecedented precision" to a more conservative "accurately quantify," clarifying the claim and reducing its strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Refined machine learning-based movement analysis revealed specific deficits in mice after cortical and striatal ischemia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Refined movement analysis reveals specific deficits in two widely-used mouse stroke models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims communicate the same result that refined analysis reveals specific deficits in two common mouse stroke models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pellet retrieval was altered in both MCAO and PT cases but did not correlate with stroke volume or ischemia extent. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Forepaw slips and relative reaching success correlate with increasing cortical lesion size in both MCAO and PT models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports correlations between behavioral measures and lesion size, which contradicts the preprint's statement of no correlation for pellet retrieval; the claim appears revised and strengthened in the published set of claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cortical ischemia was characterized by increased hand slips and modified reaching success. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Forepaw slips and relative reaching success correlate with increasing cortical lesion size in both MCAO and PT models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's description of cortical ischemia effects (hand slips, reaching success) aligns with the publication's report that these measures correlate with lesion size.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Striatal ischemia led to progressively prolonged reach durations, mirroring delayed symptom onset in basal ganglia strokes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Striatal lesion size after MCAO is associated with prolonged reach durations that occur with delayed symptom onset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same association between striatal lesion size and delayed/prolonged reach durations described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study catalyzed 284 antibacterial ketone intermediates, revealing distinct patterns for both medium-chain dehydrogenases (MDRs) and short-chain dehydrogenases (SDRs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current study demonstrates a unique pattern of activity for 284 antibacterial ketone intermediates against medium-chain and short-chain alcohol dehydrogenases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same finding about activity patterns for the 284 compounds across MDRs and SDRs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques were effectively leveraged to discriminate between compounds influenced by different alcohol dehydrogenase superfamilies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An ensemble of machine learning techniques, including Partial Least Squares regression, k-Nearest Neighbors regression, and Support Vector Machine regression, was successfully utilized to match different alcohol dehydrogenases with their ideal substrates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more specific methodological detail (specific algorithms and ensemble approach) while preserving the original ML-based discrimination/matching claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification strategy developed in this study serves as a potent approach for discerning substrates suitable for various alcohol dehydrogenases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This classification approach mitigates the reliance on high-throughput screening or engineering to identify the optimal enzyme for specific substrates. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the practical benefit of the classification strategy (reducing reliance on HTS/engineering), which clarifies the application-oriented conclusion of the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The assimilation of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) improved the precision and accuracy of compound classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assimilation of Principal Component Analysis augmented precision and accuracy in the classification of diverse compound classes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state that PCA improved precision and accuracy in classification; the publication reiterates this result (with slightly different wording).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Over 900 proteins were newly implicated in the resistance to oxidative stress. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Over 900 proteins were newly implicated in the resistance to oxidative stress. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim reproduces the preprint's result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analyses exposed phenotypes for 3492 mutants, including 124 mutants of ‘priority unstudied’ proteins conserved in humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study exposed phenotypes for 3492 mutants, including 124 mutants of ‘priority unstudied’ proteins conserved in humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim matches the preprint's quantitative phenotype discovery statement exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We obtained 56,594 high-scoring Gene Ontology (GO) predictions, of which 22,060 also featured high information content. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the specific counts of GO predictions reported in the preprint, indicating this numeric claim was omitted from the highlighted published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrated analyses revealed 1,675 novel GO predictions for 783 genes, including 47 predictions for 23 priority unstudied proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrated analyses revealed 1675 novel GO predictions for 783 genes, including 47 predictions for 23 priority unstudied proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same integrated-analysis GO prediction counts and targets as reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best scFv generated from our method represents a 28.8-fold improvement in binding over the best scFv from the directed evolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best scFv generated from our method represents a 28.7-fold improvement in binding over the best scFv from the directed evolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published numeric (28.7 vs 28.8) is effectively the same result; the claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 99% of the designed scFvs in our most successful library are improvements over the initial candidate scFv. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 99% of designed scFvs in our most successful library are improvements over the initial candidate scFv. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this quantitative result exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method demonstrates the ability to explore tradeoffs between library success and diversity during the design phase and prior to experimental testing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method demonstrates the ability to explore tradeoffs between library success and diversity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published claim preserves the core conclusion about exploring tradeoffs but omits the explicit "during the design phase and prior to experimental testing" phrase, a slight clarification of wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of our work highlight the significant impact machine learning models can have on scFv development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results of our work highlight the significant impact machine learning models can have on scFv development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's conclusion about the impact of ML on scFv development.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We expect our end-to-end method to be broadly applicable and able to provide value to other protein engineering tasks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We expect our method to be broadly applicable and provide value to other protein engineering tasks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claim retains the expectation of broad applicability and value for other protein engineering tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unsupervised machine learning can be used to deconvolve time-dependent gene expression measurements into underlying temporal components. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the use of unsupervised machine learning to deconvolve a series of time-dependent gene expression measurements into its underlying temporal components. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with equivalent scope and certainty, only changing tone to a demonstrative result statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two temporal components with oscillatory behavior correspond to the G1-S and G2-M phases of the cell cycle. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified two temporal components with oscillatory behavior, corresponding to the G1-S and G2-M phases of the cell cycle. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A third temporal component with a transient expression pattern is associated with the immediate-early response gene program, regulation of cell proliferation, and cervical cancer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A third component with a transient expression pattern is associated with the immediate early response gene program, regulation of cell proliferation, and cervical cancer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is essentially identical in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study has potential applications for early detection and monitoring of diseases and recovery processes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates potential applications of unsupervised machine learning for early detection and monitoring of diseases and recovery processes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published wording preserves the same claimed potential applications and the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study improves prenylation predictions by using a machine learning approach based on both canonical and recently identified shunted motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study improves prenylation predictions by using a machine learning approach based on both canonical and recently identified shunted motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research determines the full scope of prenylation potential among the 8000 possible Cxxx sequence combinations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research determines the full scope of prenylation potential among the 8000 possible Cxxx sequence combinations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in the publication; scope and certainty are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning approach expands the repertoire of predicted FTase targets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning-based approach expands the repertoire of predicted FTase targets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution but presents it with higher certainty, so the claim is amplified rather than changed in content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In silico predictions were validated using in vivo methods with two yeast reporters representing canonical and shunted CaaX motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In silico predictions were validated using in vivo methods with two yeast reporters representing canonical and shunted CaaX motifs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation claim is preserved in content but reported with lower certainty in the publication, indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Microbiome-based machine learning models could accurately classify PD patients within most studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Microbiome-based machine learning models accurately classify Parkinson's disease patients with an average AUC of 71.9%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a quantitative performance metric (AUC) that clarifies the preprint's general accuracy claim without changing its substantive meaning or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models were study-specific and did not generalise well across other studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning models are study-specific and do not generalize well across other studies, with an average AUC of 61%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an average cross-study performance metric that clarifies the original claim while preserving its meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training models on multiple datasets improved their general applicability and disease specificity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training models on multiple datasets improves their generalizability and disease specificity, achieving an average LOSO AUC of 68%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication supplies quantitative evidence and upgrades certainty, so the claim is clarified and its confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Meta-analysis of shotgun metagenomes delineated PD-associated microbial pathways potentially contributing to the deterioration of gut health. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Meta-analysis of shotgun metagenomes delineates PD-associated microbial pathways that may contribute to gut health deterioration. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same meta-analytic finding with similar hedging language and comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diverse microbial pathways for the biotransformation of solvents and pesticides were enriched in PD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Microbial pathways for solvent and pesticide biotransformation are enriched in Parkinson's disease. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication reports this with lower certainty, so the claim is effectively tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed a machine learning pipeline called Single cell Predictive markers (SPmarker) to identify novel cell-type marker genes in the Arabidopsis root. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have developed a machine learning pipeline called single-cell predictive marker (SPmarker) to identify novel cell-type marker genes in the Arabidopsis root. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the development of SPmarker with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method can assign cell types based on cells that were labeled using published methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have demonstrated that our method can assign cell types based on cells that were labelled using published methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result is reported in the publication with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using SPmarker, we have identified hundreds of new marker genes that were not identified before. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using SPmarker, we have identified hundreds of new marker genes that were not identified before. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively the same as the preprint claim with only minor wording differences (4D vs four-dimensional).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hippocampal neurons treated with an intracellular cholesterol transport inhibitor mimicking Niemann-Pick disease type C show that synaptic boutons significantly increase in size compared to untreated controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hippocampal neurons treated with an intracellular cholesterol transport inhibitor mimicking Niemann-Pick disease type C show that synaptic boutons significantly increase in size compared to untreated controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same experimental result with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The increase in size of synaptic boutons leads to a loss in neuronal signalling capacity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The increase in size of synaptic boutons leads to a loss in neuronal signaling capacity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved verbatim (minor British/American spelling difference) and retains the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method enables label-free segmentation of cell nuclei in optical diffraction tomography images using a deep learning framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method of label-free segmentation of cell nuclei utilizes a deep learning framework. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same core contribution: label-free nucleus segmentation implemented with a deep learning framework.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method was validated through cross-modality and cross-laboratory experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nucleus volumes from the deep learning-based label-free optical diffraction tomography segmentation accurately agreed with those from fluorescent-based methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports cross-modality agreement (ODT vs fluorescence) but does not report cross-laboratory validation, narrowing the original validation claim and tempering its breadth.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for precise cell nucleus segmentation in two, three, and four-dimensional label-free imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 4D cell nucleus segmentation was successfully performed for the time-lapse optical diffraction tomography images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication demonstrates successful 4D/time-lapse segmentation specifically, whereas the preprint made a broader claim across 2D, 3D, and 4D; the claim is thus narrowed to 4D evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework developed in this work is publicly available and has broad and immediate biomedical applications. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state that the framework is publicly available nor asserts the broad/immediate applications claim as written in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method enables long-term imaging of live cells without the use of exogenous agents. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The final paper highlights long-term label-free live-cell imaging; this specific framing was not explicitly present in the preprint text provided, so it is treated as an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study identifies four distinct cognitive profiles among struggling learners using unsupervised machine learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identifies four distinct cognitive profiles among struggling learners using unsupervised machine learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim verbatim in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Children with working memory problems and those with phonological difficulties exhibit similar learning profiles, both performing around 1 SD below age-expected levels on all learning measures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Children with working memory problems and those with phonological difficulties exhibited similar learning profiles, both performing around 1 SD below age-expected levels on all learning measures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated in the publication with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A child's cognitive profile is not predicted by their diagnosis or referral reason. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A child's cognitive profile was not predicted by diagnosis or referral reason. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion/result about lack of prediction by diagnosis or referral reason.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct patterns of brain organization were identified for each cognitive group through the construction of whole-brain structural connectomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct patterns of brain organization were identified for each cognitive group through the construction of whole-brain structural connectomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the connectome-based group differences described in the preprint without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study represents a novel approach to identifying data-driven neurocognitive dimensions underlying learning-related difficulties in a representative sample of poor learners. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study represents a novel move toward identifying data-driven neurocognitive dimensions underlying learning-related difficulties in a representative sample of poor learners. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly rephrases the contribution ("move toward" vs "approach") but preserves the same intent and level of certainty; wording is marginally more cautious.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SpaCCC facilitates the inference of spatially resolved cell-cell communications using a fine-tuned single-cell LLM and functional gene interaction network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SpaCCC facilitates the inference of spatially resolved cell-cell communications using a fine-tuned single-cell LLM and functional gene interaction network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The benchmarked performance of SpaCCC demonstrates remarkable superiority over other methods in analyzing real single-cell spatial transcriptomic datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The benchmarked performance of SpaCCC is superior to other methods when evaluated on real single-cell spatial transcriptomic datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same performance superiority claim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SpaCCC infers known ligand-receptor pairs that are concealed by existing aggregative methods and identifies communication patterns for specific cell types and their signalling pathways. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SpaCCC can infer known Ligand-Receptor pairs that are concealed by existing aggregative methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly confirms the ligand–receptor inference component of the combined preprint claim; this alignment treats that subclaim as clarified in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SpaCCC infers known ligand-receptor pairs that are concealed by existing aggregative methods and identifies communication patterns for specific cell types and their signalling pathways. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SpaCCC identifies communication patterns for specific cell types and their signaling pathways. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication also confirms the communication-patterns/ pathway-identification aspect of the preprint claim; the preprint combined these points, so both publication claims align with different facets of P2.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SpaCCC provides various cell-cell communication visualization results at both single-cell and cell type resolution. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present or emphasize a visualization-results contribution as described in the preprint, so this preprint-specific contribution appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed camera trap with integrated artificial intelligence can send real-time information from anywhere in the world to end-users. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant technical barriers exist in transmitting images for real-time analysis in areas without reliable cellular or WiFi connectivity. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's claim of real-time global transmission is tempered by the publication's explicit acknowledgment of connectivity barriers, narrowing the operational scope and reducing confidence in the 'anywhere' capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system can operate for a minimum of three months without intervention while capturing a median of 17.23 images per day. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modified camera trap system can operate for a minimum of 3 months without intervention while capturing a median of 17.23 images per day. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The operational-duration and image-rate result is retained exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median time-difference between image capture and receiving an alert was 7.35 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The median time-difference between image capture and receiving an alert was 7.35 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The latency statistic is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Excluding 'uncertain' labels and labelling consecutive series of images with the most frequent class can improve accuracy and interpretation of alerts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present this specific post-processing/labeling recommendation as a highlighted conclusion, so the claim appears omitted from the final text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The solutions presented can inform future advances in real-time ecosystem monitoring applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system's potential can be expanded for real-time biodiversity monitoring, wild resource management, and detecting illegal human activities in protected areas. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives concrete application areas (biodiversity monitoring, resource management, illegal-activity detection), which clarifies and specifies the preprint's broader statement about informing future advances.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new algorithm efficiently simulates random models of large neural networks with time asynchrony.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new algorithm efficiently simulates random models of large neural networks with time asynchrony.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim with no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model parameters are comparable to those of a small mammalian brain or human brain areas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model parameters are comparable to those of a small mammalian brain or human brain areas.
alignments.rationale: The result about parameter comparability appears identical in the published version with no added qualification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational and memory complexities of the algorithm are theoretically linear with the number of neurons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational and memory complexities of the algorithm are theoretically linear with the number of neurons.
alignments.rationale: The theoretical complexity claim is restated without modification in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results are experimentally validated by simulations involving millions of neurons and billions of synapses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results are experimentally validated by simulations of millions of neurons and billions of synapses running in a few minutes.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same validation claim but adds a runtime detail ('running in a few minutes'), which clarifies and strengthens the practical performance assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The least microenvironmental uncertainty principle (LEUP) serves as a generative model of collective migration without the need for full mechanistic details.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The least microenvironmental uncertainty principle (LEUP) serves as a generative model of collective migration without the need for precise mechanistic details.
alignments.rationale: Wording in the publication softens 'full' to 'precise' mechanistic details but conveys the same core contribution; this is a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The famous Vicsek model is a special case of the least microenvironmental uncertainty principle (LEUP).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The famous Vicsek model is demonstrated to be a special case of the least microenvironmental uncertainty principle (LEUP).
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the Vicsek model is a special case of LEUP; the publication frames it as demonstrated but does not change the substantive conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LEUP was applied to quantitatively study the collective behavior of spherical Serratia marcescens bacteria.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LEUP is applied to construct a model of the collective behavior of spherical Serratia marcescens bacteria, despite the underlying migration mechanisms being largely unknown.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the application claim but adds the context that underlying mechanisms are largely unknown, clarifying the conditions of the modelling effort.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of AlphaFold solved the computational challenge of predicting protein structures from amino acid sequences with unprecedented accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of AlphaFold solved the challenge of predicting protein structure from amino acid sequences with unprecedented accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the high-level claim about AlphaFold's transformative impact with equivalent wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed shallow, unsupervised model based on Direct Coupling Analysis (DCA) can extract structural determinants such as the contact map of protein families.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's parameters are notably fewer than those in standard DCA-based algorithms and can be used for extracting structural determinants such as the contact map of the protein family.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly compares parameter counts to DCA and retains the contact-map extraction claim, adding specificity about parameter efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's self-attention decomposition facilitates the definition of a multi-family learning architecture, allowing integration of information across multiple protein families.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The functional form of the energy function of the model allows for a multi-family learning strategy, integrating information across multiple protein families.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the mechanism (energy function form) enabling multi-family learning; wording is more formal and the claim's certainty is higher in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The autoregressive generative version of the model can efficiently generate new proteins in silico while reproducing summary statistics of the original protein family.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A generative version of the model using an autoregressive architecture is capable of efficiently generating new proteins in silico.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the generative/autoregressive claim but omits the specific assertion about reproducing summary statistics; overall certainty is reduced, so the claim is revised and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the Attention-Based DCA architecture is evaluated using Multiple Sequence Alignments (MSAs) with structural data from the Pfam database.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study investigates a single-layer unsupervised model based on the attention mechanism that mimics the attention mechanism of several popular Transformer architectures.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the investigated model at a higher level (single-layer attention relevant to Transformers) and omits the explicit Pfam/MSA evaluation detail present in the preprint; this is a partial reframing/clarification of the experimental focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structure of the task and the reward history significantly influenced both choice and response vigour in rhesus monkeys.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structure of the task and the reward history significantly influenced both choice and response vigour in rhesus monkeys.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this empirical finding verbatim; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Choices were made according to a combination of model-free and model-sensitive strategies, with a dominant influence of a particular form of model sensitivity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Choices were made according to a combination of model-free and model-sensitive strategies, with a dominant influence of a particular form of model sensitivity.
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same conclusion about the mixture of strategies and the dominant model-sensitive influence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new combined reinforcement learning model was developed to incorporate a particular credit assignment weighting procedure based on the residuals from the analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new combined reinforcement learning model was developed to account for the residuals from the analysis, incorporating a particular credit assignment weighting procedure.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the development claim but lowers certainty (publication labels contribution as medium); wording shifts to emphasize accounting for residuals, so the claim is clarified and its stated confidence is reduced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Response vigor exhibited a subtly different collection of model-free and model-sensitive influences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Response vigor exhibited a subtly different collection of model-free and model-sensitive influences.
alignments.rationale: The description of response vigor influences is preserved without meaningful change between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nervous system optimizes for energy using a prediction of the optimal gait.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nervous system optimizes for energy using a prediction of the optimal gait.
alignments.rationale: The core conclusion about predictive energy-optimization in gait control is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most participants preferred to exploit their originally preferred, but now suboptimal, gait.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most participants preferred to exploit their originally preferred, but now suboptimal, gait.
alignments.rationale: The behavioral result about participant preference for exploiting original gait appears identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Providing participants with the experience of lower cost gaits can reliably initiate optimization in those who exploit their original gait.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants with high natural gait variability were able to explore new energetic landscapes and initiate energy optimization spontaneously.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the mechanism: the preprint claims an intervention (experience of lower-cost gaits) initiates optimization broadly, whereas the publication emphasizes spontaneous initiation tied to high natural variability, narrowing the affected subgroup and revising the causal claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nervous system employs a local search process to converge on the new optimum gait over tens of seconds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nervous system employs a local search process to converge on the new optimum gait over tens of seconds after optimization is initiated.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier 'after optimization is initiated,' clarifying the temporal condition but retaining the substantive local-search claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimization process modeled as reinforcement learning closely matches experimental observations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nervous system learned to predict the new optimal gait and rapidly returned to it within a few steps if perturbed away.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more specific observed behavior (learned prediction and rapid reversion) that exemplifies the preprint's broader claim that an RL model matches observations; the mapping is plausible though the published phrasing is more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hi-C-LSTM produces low-dimensional latent representations that summarize intra-chromosomal Hi-C contacts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hi-C-LSTM produces low-dimensional latent representations that summarize intra-chromosomal Hi-C contacts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in wording and meaning to the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These representations contain all the information needed to recreate the original Hi-C matrix with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representations generated by Hi-C-LSTM contain all the information needed to recreate the observed Hi-C matrix with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about reconstruction fidelity with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hi-C-LSTM outperforms existing methods in recreating the original Hi-C matrix. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hi-C-LSTM outperforms existing methods in recreating the Hi-C matrix. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is restated in the publication with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representations enable the identification of a variety of conformation-defining genomic elements. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representations enable the identification of conformation-defining genomic elements, including nuclear compartments and conformation-related transcription factors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method allows for in-silico perturbation experiments that measure the influence of cis-regulatory elements on conformation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hi-C-LSTM enables in-silico perturbation experiments that measure the influence of cis-regulatory elements on conformation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational optimization method discovers a four-dimensional transcription factor network that maintains a circadian rhythm over many days. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimization method successfully discovers a four-dimensional transcription factor network that maintains a circadian rhythm over many days. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimized transcription factor network successfully buffers strong stochastic perturbations in molecular dynamics. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transcription factor network can entrain to an external day-night signal that randomly turns on and off at intervals of several days. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TF network can entrain to an external day-night signal that randomly turns on and off at intervals of several days. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WorMachine enables the extraction of morphological features and quantification of fluorescent signals. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WorMachine demonstrates the ability to score binary and continuous sexual phenotypes, quantify the effects of different RNAi treatments, and measure intercellular protein aggregation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: WorMachine provides accurate and reproducible analysis for measuring diverse phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WorMachine is a high-throughput, automated, and unbiased analysis tool for measuring phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TEA-GCN outperforms the state-of-the-art in predicting gene functions and inferring gene regulatory networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TEA-GCN outperforms the state-of-the-art in predicting gene functions and inferring gene regulatory networks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: TEA-GCN can identify tissue-/condition-specific co-expression in biologically-relevant dataset partitions with high co-expression. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TEA-GCNs exhibit enhanced conservation across species, making them suitable for multi-species comparative studies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TEA-GCNs exhibit enhanced conservation across species, making them suitable for multi-species comparative studies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multimodal database includes task-based and resting state fMRI, structural MRI, and EEG data from participants over 65 years old. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multimodal database includes task-based and resting state fMRI, structural MRI, and EEG data from participants over 65 years old. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The database provides comprehensive data on participants' language history, lifetime experiences, linguistic and cognitive skills. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: ETNA's embeddings can be used to transfer genetic interactions across species and identify phenotypic alignments.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach lays the groundwork for potential opportunities for drug repurposing and translational studies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The enhanced COVID-19 dataset contains 183 samples and 43 features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computationally derived EQ and vector representations were comparably successful in recapitulating biological truth to representations created through manual EQ statement curation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computationally derived EQ and vector representations were comparably successful in recapitulating biological truth to representations created through manual EQ statement curation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP methods for generating vector representations of phenotypes are scalable to large quantities of text because they require no human input.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is now possible to computationally and automatically produce and populate large-scale information resources that enable researchers to query phenotypic descriptions directly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is now possible to computationally and automatically produce and populate large-scale information resources that enable researchers to query phenotypic descriptions directly.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward this conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep machine learning can uncover evolutionary relationships directly from protein sequences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The largest ESM2 model tends to outperform other protein-based language models in recovering phylogenetic relationships.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The largest ESM2 model tends to outperform other pLMs by recovering phylogenetic relationships among homologous protein sequences in both low- and high-gap settings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SleepSatelightFTC achieves an accuracy of 84.8% and a kappa coefficient of 0.787 on the Sleep-EDF-78 dataset.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clair3-Trio employs a Trio-to-Trio deep neural network model to improve variant calling accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clair3-Trio employs a Trio-to-Trio deep neural network model to improve variant calling accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological/result claim with no substantive change in wording or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clair3-Trio predicted far fewer Mendelian inheritance violation variations than current state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clair3-Trio predicted far fewer Mendelian inheritance violation variations than current state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about reduced Mendelian violations is carried over unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Trio-to-Trio model of Clair3-Trio is more accurate than competing architectures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Trio-to-Trio model demonstrated greater accuracy than competing architectures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is slightly rephrased but the claim of superior accuracy relative to other architectures is the same with equal certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed cryoET processing pipeline leveraging convolutional neural networks improved the throughput of cryoET analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of convolutional neural networks and cryo-electron tomography improved the throughput of cryoET analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same claim about improved throughput using CNNs and cryoET with equivalent certainty; wording is slightly different but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cryoET processing pipeline accurately identified viral components within tomograms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed pipeline accurately identified viral components within tomograms of the A/Puerto Rico/8/34 (H1N1) influenza strain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the strain (H1N1 A/Puerto Rico/8/34), narrowing the original general claim to a concrete dataset while retaining the accuracy claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of this processing pipeline can aid in the structural characterization of influenza viruses and other pleomorphic viruses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The processing pipeline can aid in the structural characterization of other pleomorphic viruses and infected cells. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis: it removes explicit mention of influenza viruses (covered elsewhere) and adds 'infected cells', changing the intended applicability; overall the conclusion is altered rather than preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study successfully characterized influenza viral morphology and glycoprotein density. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach successfully characterized influenza morphology and glycoprotein density. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about characterizing influenza morphology and glycoprotein density with equivalent certainty; wording is slightly rephrased.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extended-alphabet molecular storage paradigm offers a nearly two-fold increase in storage density. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extended molecular alphabet may potentially offer a nearly 2-fold increase in storage density and potentially the same order of reduction in the recording latency. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication hedges the claim (from a high-confidence result to a low-confidence potential), softening the certainty while retaining the same nominal magnitude of benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extended-alphabet molecular storage system potentially reduces the recording time by the same order as the increase in storage density. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extended molecular alphabet may potentially offer a nearly 2-fold increase in storage density and potentially the same order of reduction in the recording latency. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same potential reduction in recording latency but with lower certainty, shifting language to a more cautious phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A neural network architecture designed to classify raw current signals ensures an average accuracy exceeding 60%, which is 39 times higher than that of random guessing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Single-molecule sequencing of the extended alphabet using a neural network architecture classifies raw current signals generated by Oxford Nanopore sequencers with an average accuracy exceeding 60%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the performance result (accuracy >60%) for the neural-network classifier on raw current signals with comparable framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of modified nucleotides do not induce dramatic disruption of the DNA double helix, making the extended alphabet system potentially compatible with PCR-based random access data retrieval. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of modified nucleotides lead to only minor perturbations of the DNA double helix. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the biophysical observation (minor perturbations) but omits the applied inference about PCR compatibility, narrowing the claim to an empirical result and removing the application-oriented conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We experimentally demonstrate a broadly applicable single layer ANN type architecture with molecular engineered bacteria to perform complex irreversible computing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We experimentally demonstrate a broadly applicable single layer ANN type architecture with molecular engineered bacteria to perform complex irreversible computing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this experimental demonstration verbatim with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The encoder and majority functions and reversible computing were experimentally implemented within living cells for the first time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The encoder and majority functions and reversible computing were experimentally implemented within living cells for the first time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the novel implementation claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We established a set of rules by correlating truth tables, mathematical equations of ANN, and molecular-device design. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We established a set of rules by correlating truth tables, mathematical equations of ANNs, and cellular device design. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Wording is slightly adjusted ('molecular-device' to 'cellular device') but the contribution and certainty remain the same; the change refines terminology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first adaptation of ANN type architecture with engineered cells. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first adaptation of ANN type architecture with engineered cells. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is preserved verbatim with identical certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work may have significance in new platform for biomolecular computing, reversible computing and in transforming living cells as ANN-enabled hardware. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work may have significance in establishing a new platform for cellular computing and reversible computing. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down ambition and certainty, removes explicit mention of 'transforming living cells as ANN-enabled hardware', and narrows the framing to 'cellular computing' with lower stated confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interleaved learning promotes the formation of representations that directly link across related experiences in humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interleaved learning promotes the formation of representations that directly link across related experiences in humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical finding identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interleaved learning leads to fast and automatic recognition of item relatedness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interleaved learning leads to fast and automatic recognition of item relatedness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interleaved learning affords efficient generalization and is critical for inference when learning requires statistical integration of noisy information over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interleaved learning affords efficient generalization and is critical for inference when learning requires statistical integration of noisy information over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this result without alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate the power of interleaved learning and implicate the formation of integrated, distributed representations that support generalization in humans. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate the power of interleaved learning and implicate the formation of integrated, distributed representations that support generalization in humans. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-level conclusion identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean area under the receiver operating curve for detecting acute ischemic stroke (AIS) was 0.963±0.0520 across all models. (certainty=high, type=result)
