alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean AUROC for detecting acute ischemic stroke (AIS) was 0.963 ± 0.0520 across all models evaluated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Texts are effectively identical, reporting the same AUROC and uncertainty; no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Logistic regression classifiers with L1 penalty provided the best performance in identifying AIS patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report that L1-penalized logistic regression was best; instead it emphasizes a best-performing case/control labeling and F1 score, so the algorithm-specific claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The top probabilities from a model-predicted AIS cohort were significantly enriched for self-reported AIS patients without AIS diagnosis codes, with a fold increase of 65-250 over expected. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The top probabilities from a model-predicted AIS cohort were significantly enriched for AIS patients without AIS diagnosis codes, with an enrichment factor of 60–150 fold over expected. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim of strong enrichment is retained but the reported enrichment range has been reduced and narrowed in the publication, reflecting a revision and tempering of magnitude.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms can accurately identify AIS patients without relying on diagnosis codes or using process-intensive manual feature curation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms provide a generalizable method to accurately identify AIS patients without the need for process-intensive manual feature curation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the conclusion but frames it as a generalizable method and expresses higher certainty, clarifying and broadening the claim while increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further external validation of the machine learning models is needed to confirm their generalizability to other academic institutions. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The explicit limitation calling for external validation is present in the preprint but is not reflected in the publication claims provided, so it appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our modelling approach provided enhanced predictive performance compared to more traditional approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our modelling approach provided enhanced predictive performance compared to more traditional approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in preprint and publication with no substantive change in wording or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We were able to efficiently capture and visualise strong non-linear patterns in shaping exposure risk from CDV and feline parvovirus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach captures and visualizes strong nonlinear patterns and models complex interactions between variables in shaping exposure risk. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the core claim about capturing nonlinear patterns but clarifies that it also models complex interactions and generalizes beyond the two pathogens, broadening the methodological description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lions were more likely to be exposed to CDV at a young age but only in low rainfall years. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We found that lions were more likely to be exposed to CDV at a young age but only in low rainfall years. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The substantive epidemiological finding is identical; the publication presents it with higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our framework has the potential to aid in predicting disease risk in animal populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our framework has the potential to aid in predicting disease risk in animal populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is verbatim and unchanged in scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can be used to build robust predictive models suitable for other ecological applications. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework can be used to build robust predictive models suitable for other ecological applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the contribution/utility claim with similar wording and certainty; classification unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed machine learning algorithm demonstrated an AUROC of 0.88, sensitivity of 77.7%, and specificity of 79.9% on excluded test data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The final algorithm demonstrated an AUROC of 0.88, sensitivity of 77.7%, and a specificity of 79.9% on excluded test data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical performance metrics and wording between preprint and publication; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The specificity of the algorithm was 40.2% for 100% sensitivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The specificity of the algorithm was 40.2% for 100% sensitivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match of the reported trade-off metric; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm achieved sound and high prognostic performance to predict AD conversion using easily clinically derived information. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm achieved sound and high prognostic performance to predict AD conversion using easily clinically derived information. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion retained verbatim with same certainty; no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm has beneficial application to improve recruitment in clinical trials and to more selectively prescribe new and newly emerging early interventions to high AD risk patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm indicates beneficial application to improve recruitment in clinical trials and to more selectively prescribe new and newly emerging early interventions to high AD risk patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication preserves the applied-consequence claim with only minor wording change ('has' vs 'indicates'), so semantic intent is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sway-metric based RF classifier had high classification accuracy in discriminating controls from MS individuals, achieving over 86% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sway-metric based random forest classifier had high accuracy in discriminating controls from MS individuals, achieving over 86% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result statement is effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sway sample entropy was identified as the strongest feature for classification of low-risk MS individuals from healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sway sample entropy was identified as the strongest feature for classification of low-risk MS individuals from healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same feature importance claim in both versions; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mediolateral sway amplitude was identified as the strongest predictor for fall risk groupings in all other comparisons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mediolateral sway amplitude was identified as the strongest predictor for fall risk groupings in all other comparisons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical predictive-feature claim across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings may set the foundation for the development of guidelines for reporting balance impairment in individuals with MS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include this forward-looking statement about guideline development, indicating it was omitted from the final claims set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Viral load and age were identified as the most important predictors of death in Ebola virus disease patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Age and viral load contributed more than 5-fold the weighting of other features in the prognostic model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the same ordering of predictors but quantifies their dominance (>5-fold), thereby clarifying and strengthening the numerical characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parsimonious model generated from the study achieved an Area Under the Curve (AUC) ranging from 0.7 to 0.8 and an overall accuracy of 64% to 74%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parsimonious model approached the predictive power of observational assessments by experienced clinicians with an AUC of 0.70-0.79 and accuracy of 0.64-0.74. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics are effectively identical; publication additionally contextualizes the result versus clinicians but does not change the core numeric claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine-learning models and mHealth tools have the potential to improve the standard of care in low-resource settings and emergency scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinically guided prognostic models can recapitulate clinical expertise and be useful when such expertise is unavailable. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the potential benefit more specifically as recapitulating clinical expertise and usefulness when expertise is unavailable, which clarifies and narrows the mechanism relative to the broader wording in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of prognostic models with mobile technologies can facilitate access to comprehensive bodies of medical knowledge. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating prognostic models into mHealth tools may facilitate their interpretation and provide informed access to comprehensive clinical guidelines. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the claim about mHealth integration facilitating access but specifies interpretation and clinical guidelines; certainty is slightly reduced in the publication, so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The program vHULK achieved 99% accuracy at the genus level and 98% accuracy at the species level in random controlled test sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: vHULK obtained on average 83% precision and 79% recall at the genus level, and 71% precision and 67% recall at the species level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different, lower performance metrics (precision/recall rather than the very high accuracy numbers reported in the preprint), indicating the original performance claim was revised and toned down.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In a validation dataset with 2,178 phage genomes, vHULK obtained mean accuracies of 82% at the genus level and 52% at the species level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: vHULK obtained on average 83% precision and 79% recall at the genus level, and 71% precision and 67% recall at the species level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report validation-set performance but use different metrics and give different numbers; the publication revises the reported performance measures and results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: vHULK demonstrated substantially better performance compared to other phage host prediction programs on the same validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: vHULK achieved better performance at both the genus and the species levels than three other tools on a test data set with 2153 phage genomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the comparative conclusion but clarifies it (explicitly names comparison to three other tools and specifies dataset size), without changing direction of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: vHULK is a new program developed for phage host prediction based on 9,504 phage genome features. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: vHULK represents an advance on the state of art in phage host prediction. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the contribution claim but phrases it more generally as an advance on the state of the art and drops the specific feature-count detail, reducing specificity and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A self-correcting machine learning method leveraging Internet-based search activity produces reliable and timely flu estimates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A self-correcting machine learning method, leveraging Internet-based disease-related search activity and historical flu trends, has the potential to produce reliable and timely flu estimates in multiple Latin American countries. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core result but softens it to a potential and explicitly notes incorporation of historical trends; this clarifies/tones the original definitive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology is applicable in multiple Latin American countries. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ARGO-like models' predictive power outperform autoregressive models in 6 out of 8 countries in the 2012-2016 time period. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies geographic applicability (performance in 6/8 countries), providing empirical support that clarifies and strengthens the preprint's general applicability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A real-time methodology for monitoring flu activity in middle income countries that is accurate and generalizable has not yet been presented. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this limitation or explicitly claim novelty relative to prior methods in the same negative formulation, so the preprint's explicit 'has not yet been presented' limitation is not preserved.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ARGO significantly improves on historical flu estimates produced by the now discontinued GFT for the time period of 2012-2015. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific comparative result (improvement over historical GFT estimates for 2012-2015) is presented in the publication but has no direct counterpart in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our methodology generally outperforms both the now-discontinued tool GFT, and autoregressive methodologies that exploit only historical flu activity to produce future disease estimates. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit broad comparative conclusion about outperforming GFT and autoregressive approaches that was not separately stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified 12,207 eQTLs from 558 transcriptional profiles of lint-bearing ovules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 12,207 expression quantitative trait loci (eQTLs) are identified from 558 transcriptional profiles of lint-bearing ovules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative result with identical sample numbers and eQTL count.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 66 out of 187 known phenotypic GWAS loci are colocalized with 1,090 eQTLs, forming 38 major functional GRNs predominantly associated with seed size-related phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sixty-six known phenotypic GWAS loci are colocalized with 1,090 eQTLs, forming 38 functional gene regulatory networks (GRNs) associated predominantly with seed yield. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same counts and GRN structure but shifts the phenotype wording to 'seed yield' (a clarified phrasing), preserving the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Narrow-sense heritability analysis showed that heritability increased when combining eQTLs with GRNs compared to previous yield trait GWAS loci. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining the eQTLs within the seed yield GRNs significantly increases the portion of narrow-sense heritability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the essence of the heritability finding, emphasizing significance of the increase when combining eQTLs in GRNs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost) machine learning approach effectively predicts seed cotton yield phenotypes based on gene expression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost) machine learning approach is applied to predict seed cotton yield phenotypes based on gene expression. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports application of XGBoost to prediction (less strongly worded about effectiveness), so the contribution is preserved but the claim about predictive effectiveness is presented with more modest framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides insights into the mining of GRNs in relation to the pleiotropy of phenotype. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Top-ranking eGenes (NF-YB3, FLA2, and GRDP1) derived with pleiotropic effects on yield traits are validated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication supplies concrete validation of specific eGenes with pleiotropic effects, which clarifies and strengthens the preprint's higher-level claim about insights into pleiotropy and GRN mining.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several large-scale datasets frequently used in the domain of machine learning are far from a uniform coverage of known biomolecular structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many widely-used datasets lack uniform coverage of biomolecular structures, limiting the predictive power of models trained on them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about non-uniform coverage of biomolecular structures in commonly used datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of uniform coverage in training data severely confines the predictive power of models trained on this data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many widely-used datasets lack uniform coverage of biomolecular structures, limiting the predictive power of models trained on them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the coverage and its effect on predictive power into one claim, preserving the preprint's conclusion about constrained model performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An exact approach combining Integer Linear Programming and intricate heuristic bounds ensures efficient computations and dependable results for measuring molecular structure similarity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce an efficient approach combining Integer Linear Programming and heuristic bounds to address the computational hardness of the proposed method. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same ILP+heuristic approach to tackle computational hardness, matching the preprint's methodological contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods may allow creators of large-scale datasets to identify regions in molecular structure space where it is advisable to provide additional training data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose two additional methods to assess whether training datasets diverge from known molecular distributions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication formalizes the preprint's suggestion by proposing concrete methods to assess dataset divergence, clarifying how dataset gaps can be identified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a distance measure based on solving the Maximum Common Edge Subgraph (MCES) problem, which aligns well with chemical similarity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The MCES-based distance measure is described in the publication but was not explicitly stated in the provided preprint claims, so it is treated as an added specific methodological claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tuning for direction does not take place in a uniform fashion but in a modular manner in which some muscle groups play a primary role. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tuning for direction does not take place in a uniform fashion but in a modular manner in which some muscle groups play a primary role. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The antigravity muscles were more finely tuned to pointing direction than the gravity muscles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The antigravity muscles were more finely tuned to pointing direction than the gravity muscles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deactivation of antigravity muscles during the first half of downward pointing is tuned to pointing direction rather than being an on-off function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deactivation of antigravity muscles during the first half of downward pointing is tuned to pointing direction rather than being an on-off function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this nuanced conclusion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phasic EMG negativity is an essential feature of muscle control. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phasic EMG negativity is an essential feature of muscle control. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about phasic EMG negativity is preserved identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The motor system tunes muscle commands to exploit gravity effects and reduce muscular effort. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No matching publication claim explicitly states that the motor system exploits gravity to reduce muscular effort, so this specific conclusion is not present in the provided publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Machine Learning analysis using Linear Discriminant analysis and Support Vector Machines led to the same conclusions, demonstrating its viability for investigating motor control. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new claim about machine-learning analyses reproducing the study's conclusions, which was not in the preprint claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GENNUS introduces novel data augmentation strategies using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) to enhance ncRNA classification performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GENNUS introduces novel data augmentation strategies using generative adversarial networks (GANs) and synthetic minority over-sampling technique (SMOTE) to enhance mirtron and canonical microRNA (miRNA) classification performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution but specifies the RNA types (mirtron and canonical miRNA), making the claim more specific (clarified and narrowed).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained on a combination of real and GAN-generated data improve classification accuracy compared to traditional SMOTE techniques or only with real data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained on a combination of real and GAN-generated data improve classification accuracy compared to traditional SMOTE techniques or only with real data.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication with the same comparison and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GANs enhance model performance and provide a richer representation of minority classes, thus improving generalization capabilities across various machine learning frameworks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GANs enhance model performance and provide a richer representation of minority classes, thus improving generalization capabilities across various machine learning frameworks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion verbatim, so semantic, scope, and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work highlights the transformative potential of synthetic data generation in addressing data limitations in genomics.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a claim about the broader 'transformative potential' in genomics; instead it adds a dataset limitation claim, so the preprint's broader visionary statement is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ArtiDock is 29-38% more accurate compared to leading open-source and commercial classical docking techniques such as AutoDock, Vina, and Glide.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ArtiDock is 29-38% more accurate in comparison to leading open-source and commercial classical docking techniques such as AutoDock, Vina, and Glide.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the quantitative accuracy improvement claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ArtiDock provides a low computational cost while maintaining high accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ArtiDock demonstrates competitive accuracy at considerably higher throughput compared to a wide range of AI docking and AI cofolding methods using the PoseX benchmark.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes 'low computational cost' as 'considerably higher throughput' and benchmarks against additional AI methods, making the claim more specific and broader in comparison; publication confidence is higher.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ArtiDock excels in challenging docking scenarios involving unbound protein structures and binding sites containing ions and structured water molecules.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ArtiDock excels in challenging docking scenarios involving unbound protein structures and binding sites containing ions and structured water molecules.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result but with higher certainty (medium→high), so semantic and scope are unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ArtiDock could be considered as a method of choice in high-throughput virtual screening scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ArtiDock could be considered as a method of choice in high-throughput virtual screening scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The concluding recommendation appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for automating counting of shag nests demonstrates a high reliability and speed in estimating nest counts with an F1 score greater than 0.95.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for automating counting of shag nests demonstrates a high reliability and speed in estimating nest counts with an F1 score greater than 0.95.
alignments.rationale: The result is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained using data from only two shag colonies can generalize well to images of both spatially and temporally distinct colonies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained on data from only two shag colonies can generalize well to images of both spatially and temporally distinct colonies.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same generalization result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The practical application of this method opens the possibility for large-scale surveys of Antarctic islands to discover undiscovered shag colonies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of Remotely Piloted Aircraft Systems (RPAS) combined with machine learning algorithms can facilitate large-scale surveys of Antarctic islands for undiscovered shag colonies.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies RPAS combined with ML as the operational approach to enable large-scale surveys, narrowing the original, more general application claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study discusses the conditions for optimal performance of the model as well as its limitations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study discusses the conditions for optimal performance of the model as well as its limitations.
alignments.rationale: Discussion of model performance conditions and limitations is present in both versions without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinical data alone is sufficient to stratify individuals with and without DSPN, achieving an AUROC of 0.752.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinical data alone is sufficient to stratify individuals with and without DSPN, achieving an AUROC of 0.752.
alignments.rationale: The stratification result and reported AUROC are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicting DSPN incidence 6.5±0.2 years later strongly benefits from clinical data complemented with two or more molecular modalities, resulting in an improved ΔAUROC >0.1 and an achieved AUROC of 0.714.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicting DSPN incidence 6.5 ± 0.2 years later strongly benefits from clinical data complemented with two or more molecular modalities, resulting in an improved ΔAUROC of greater than 0.1 and an achieved AUROC of 0.714.
alignments.rationale: The predictive improvement with multimodal data and the numerical results are restated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Important and interpretable features of incident DSPN prediction include up-regulation of proinflammatory cytokines, down-regulation of the SUMOylation pathway, and essential fatty acids.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Important and interpretable features of incident DSPN prediction include up-regulation of proinflammatory cytokines, down-regulation of the SUMOylation pathway, and essential fatty acids.
alignments.rationale: The set of important features is presented the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed Interpretable Multimodal Machine Learning (IMML) framework empowers biomarker identification for DSPN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study serves as proof of concept for the utility of the Interpretable Multimodal Machine Learning framework in studying complex diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as a 'proof of concept' for utility in studying complex diseases rather than asserting that the framework 'empowers' biomarker identification, which is a more cautious characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings may yield novel insights into the disease pathophysiology and guide prevention strategies for incident DSPN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These features may become biomarkers for incident DSPN and guide prevention strategies.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the potential translational implication more specific (features becoming biomarkers) and expresses it with higher certainty (low→medium), narrowing the original broad statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neighborhood context model using the Adaboost-DT algorithm performs the best in predicting whether a disulfide bond could improve protein thermostability, with an AUC-ROC score of 0.773 and accuracy of 0.714.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neighborhood context model based on the Adaboost-DT algorithm achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.773 and an accuracy score of 0.714.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same model, metrics, and performance values.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 exhibits a high superiority in predicting disulfide bonds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 demonstrates high superiority in predicting disulfide bonds.
alignments.rationale: The claim about AlphaFold2's superior predictive ability is preserved with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The coevolutionary relationship between residue pairs could guide artificial disulfide-bond design to some extent.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state that coevolutionary relationships guide design; instead it presents experimental results and a general conclusion about disulfide bonds, so this specific mechanistic/design claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SS-bond data has been integrated into an online server named ThermoLink.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SS-bond data has been integrated into an online server named ThermoLink.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of integrating data into ThermoLink is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel cost-effective benchtop device can classify the outcome of each trial automatically with an accuracy of greater than 94% without the use of GPUs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The device described can classify the outcome of each trial with an accuracy of greater than 94% without the use of graphical processing units (GPUs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim (›94% accuracy) and the no-GPU point verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The device can train 30 mice collectively performing approximately 83,000 trials over 3 months, saving users an estimated 8 and a half hours of labour per day. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ReachingBots successfully automated SPRG training and assessment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint gives specific throughput and labor-savings numbers; the paper instead makes a general claim of successful automation without reproducing the numerical throughput or time‑savings, narrowing and moderating the original specific claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Devices measured motor deficits induced in mice by a focal ischaemic stroke, highlighting the heterogeneity in reaching outcomes following stroke. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the heterogeneity in reaching outcomes following stroke, with some mice showing large persistent deficits while others showed only transient deficits. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the heterogeneity conclusion but adds detail about persistent versus transient deficits and raises certainty, clarifying and strengthening the original observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We predict that bilateral lesions of motor cortex would cause long-lasting deficits in reach-and-grasp in mice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is conjectured that reach-and-grasp is represented in motor cortex bilaterally but with greater asymmetry in some mice than in others. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint predicts a functional deficit after bilateral lesions; the publication instead frames bilateral representation with inter-animal asymmetry, shifting from a causal prediction to a representational conjecture and softening the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Highly parallel automated training and assessment should accelerate the development of new therapies for movement disorders. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ReachingBots successfully automated SPRG training and assessment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint makes an aspirational claim about accelerating therapy development; the publication documents successful automation but does not make the downstream claim about accelerating therapeutic development, narrowing and tempering the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phage G has a 499 kbp, non-permuted, linear genome that is uniquely characterized by two pairs of ends. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phage G is one of the largest phages with a size of >0.6 µm and a 499 kbp, non-permuted, linear genome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the genome size and permutation description but omits the specific ‘‘two pairs of ends’’ detail and adds a morphological size statement, changing details while broadening context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phage G encodes over 650 protein-coding open reading frames (ORFs), with more than 65% being hypothetical proteins with no known function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phage G has >650 protein-coding open reading frames (ORFs), with >65% being hypothetical proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric ORF count and proportion of hypothetical proteins with essentially identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genome of phage G includes an expansive repertoire of auxiliary metabolic genes (AMGs) acquired from its bacterial host, which can manipulate host sporulation and antiviral escape. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phage G has acquired an expansive repertoire of auxiliary metabolic genes (AMGs) from its bacterial host, which manipulate host sporulation and include antiviral escape genes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about AMGs and their functions with minor wording differences, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methylome of phage G was localized to a cryptic region with limited functional homology, which was identified as a hot spot for methylation at 32%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this methylome localization or the 32% methylation hotspot, so the preprint methylation claim does not appear in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study represents the first comprehensive examination of the genomic repertoire of the only cultivated megaphage over five decades. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study represents a doorway into the complexity of the genomic repertoire of the only cultivated megaphage. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's strong ‘‘first comprehensive’’ and five-decade framing is toned down in the publication to a more modest characterization as a ‘‘doorway into complexity’’, narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-classical HLA plays a crucial immunomodulatory role and regulates various immune responses. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Non-classical HLA-based immunotherapies have many advantages over traditional HLA-based immunotherapy, particularly against cancer and COVID-19 infection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the broader functional role of non-classical HLA in terms of advantages for immunotherapies against specific diseases, shifting emphasis from a general immunomodulatory role to therapeutic applications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning based-models achieved more than 0.98 AUC for HLA-G alleles on validation or independent dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning models achieved more than 0.98 AUC for HLA-G alleles on the validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the >0.98 AUC result for HLA-G but specifies the validation dataset only (removing the independent dataset claim), narrowing the dataset scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our models achieved the highest AUC of 0.96 and 0.88 on the validation dataset for HLA-E*01:01 and HLA-E*01:03, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models achieved the highest AUC of 0.96 and 0.94 on the validation dataset for HLA-E*01:01 and HLA-E*01:03, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same first allele AUC but a higher AUC for HLA-E*01:03 (0.94 vs 0.88), so the numeric result is revised and the performance claim is strengthened for that allele.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a web server HLAncPred for predicting promiscuous binders for non-classical HLA alleles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a web server HLAncPred to predict promiscuous binders for the non-classical HLA alleles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same development of the HLAncPred web server for predicting promiscuous binders, unchanged in content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HLA-E restricted T-cell therapy may reduce COVID-19 associated cytokine storm. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication mentions advantages of non-classical HLA immunotherapies against COVID-19 generally but does not report the specific prediction that HLA-E–restricted T-cell therapy could reduce COVID-19 cytokine storm, so this specific prediction is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STimage is the first to thoroughly address robustness and interpretability in spatial transcriptomic models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STimage enhances robustness by estimating gene expression distributions and quantifying both data-driven and model-based uncertainty using an ensemble approach with foundation models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication documents technical approaches that address robustness (and does so in detail) but does not claim it is the "first" to thoroughly address robustness and interpretability, so the grander novelty claim is toned down.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STimage predicts gene expression based on parameter distributions rather than fixed data points, allowing for generalisation at a population scale. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STimage enhances robustness by estimating gene expression distributions and quantifying both data-driven and model-based uncertainty using an ensemble approach with foundation models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly describes estimating expression distributions and quantifying uncertainty, which clarifies the preprint's description of using distributions rather than fixed points.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STimage achieves interpretability by analysing model attribution at a single-cell level and in the context of histopathological annotation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interpretability is achieved through attribution analysis at single-cell resolution integrated with histopathological annotations, functional genes, and latent representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the single-cell attribution and histopathology integration and further specifies additional integrated elements, clarifying and expanding the interpretability description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STimage is robust to technical variations across platforms, data types, sample preservation methods, and disease types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STimage enhances robustness by estimating gene expression distributions and quantifying both data-driven and model-based uncertainty using an ensemble approach with foundation models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explains the technical mechanisms (distribution estimation, uncertainty quantification) that underlie robustness to technical variation, thus clarifying the preprint's claim about broad robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using STimage-predicted values based solely on imaging input, we could stratify patient survival groups. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STimage-predicted gene expression can stratify patient survival and predict drug response. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms survival stratification from image-predicted expression and further reports prediction of drug response with higher certainty, broadening and strengthening the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved 95% accuracy under long-day conditions using just three behavioral features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved 95% accuracy under long-day conditions using just three behavioural features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact accuracy claim under the same conditions and feature set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model maintained 76% accuracy under short-day conditions when both types of beetles are reproductively active. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model maintained 76% accuracy under short-day conditions when both types are reproductively active. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same short-day accuracy and experimental condition, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating behavioral monitoring with machine learning provides a rapid, scalable method for tracking population responses to climate change. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating behavioural monitoring with machine learning can provide a rapid, scalable method for tracking population responses to climate change. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same methodological conclusion about scalability for tracking climate‑driven population responses.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study reveals persistent behavioral differences between breeding strategies of the Asian burying beetle. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach offers novel insights into species' adaptive responses to shifting seasonal cues across different elevational gradients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the biological insight from a specific persistent behavioral difference between breeding strategies to broader insights about adaptive responses across elevational/seasonal gradients, so the focus is shifted and broadened while remaining more tentative.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XGBoost, kNN, and RF showed the strongest predictive power for immunogenicity prediction among the eight commonly-used ML methods evaluated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extreme Gradient Boosting, K Nearest Neighbours, and Random Forest showed the strongest predictive power for B cell epitope immunogenicity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent specificity (same models and prediction target), so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel soft-voting based ensemble approach, VirusImmu, demonstrated a powerful and stable capability for viral immunogenicity prediction across various test sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel soft-voting based ensemble approach VirusImmu demonstrated a powerful and stable capability for viral immunogenicity prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same claim about VirusImmu's performance with no substantive change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VirusImmu was successfully applied to identify linear B cell epitopes against African Swine Fever Virus, as confirmed by indirect ELISA in vitro. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VirusImmu was successfully applied to identify linear B cell epitopes against African Swine Fever Virus as confirmed by indirect ELISA in vitro. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same experimental validation claim verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VirusImmu exhibited tremendous potential in predicting the immunogenicity of viral protein segments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VirusImmu exhibited tremendous potentials in predicting immunogenicity of viral protein segments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same concluding statement (minor wording difference only), so the claim is unchanged in meaning and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning approaches can significantly reduce the cost of experimental work in developing novel vaccine candidates. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader conclusion about ML reducing experimental cost does not appear among the preprint claims and is introduced in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SparrKULee is a comprehensive dataset of 64-channel EEG recordings from 85 young individuals with normal hearing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SparrKULee is a Speech-evoked Auditory Repository of EEG data that includes 64-channel EEG recordings from 85 young individuals with normal hearing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more formal name/description but conveys the same core contribution; thus the claim is clarified with added detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This dataset is more extensive than any currently available dataset in terms of both the number of participants and the amount of data per participant. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset comprises recordings from participants who listened to 90–150 min of natural speech, making it more extensive than any currently available dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the 'more extensive' claim but supports it specifically with duration details; this clarifies the evidence though the overall scope of 'more extensive' remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset is suitable for training larger machine learning models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SparrKULee is suitable for training larger machine learning models due to its substantial quantity of data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same suitability claim with similar level of certainty and justification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Benchmark scores for future research are provided by evaluating the dataset using linear and state-of-the-art non-linear models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset was evaluated using linear and state-of-the-art non-linear models, providing benchmark scores for future research. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the evaluation and provision of benchmark scores with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mutations of the trigger loop (TL) in RNA Polymerase II can lead to hypo- and hyperactivity as well as altered fidelity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mutations of trigger loop residues cause distinct effects on catalysis, including hypo- and hyperactivity and altered fidelity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same catalytic effects of TL mutations with equivalent specificity and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can predict the phenotypes of TL mutations with a 0.68 R² correlation from amino acid sequences alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning could predict the phenotypes of mutants with a 0.68 R2 correlation from amino acid sequences alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the numeric predictive performance claim verbatim, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lethal mutations in the TL tend to increase distances of TL residues to the NTP substrate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A subset of loss-of-function and lethal mutations tends to increase distances of trigger loop residues to the NTP substrate. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies the effect to a subset and groups loss-of-function with lethal mutations and lowers certainty, thus narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gain-of-function (GOF) mutants generally disrupt hydrophobic contacts among the TL and nearby helices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some gain-of-function mutants appear to cause disruption of hydrophobic contacts among the trigger loop and nearby helices. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the generality ('generally' -> 'some', 'appear to'), reducing scope and confidence relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Molecular dynamics data is too noisy and possibly incomplete to directly infer functional phenotypes from machine learning predictions. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation about MD data noisiness and incompleteness is not present in the publication claims and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ferrets could accurately perform the task across all pitch-shifted conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ferrets could accurately perform the task across all pitch-shifted conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this experimental result exactly, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shifting the fundamental frequency (F0) elevated false alarms and extended reaction times. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shifting the fundamental frequency (F0) within trials elevated false alarms and extended reaction times. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same finding with similar detail and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spectrotemporal similarity of target and non-target words was the strongest predictor of the likelihood of false alarming. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spectrotemporal similarity of target and non-target words was the strongest predictor of the likelihood of false alarming. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is presented identically in the publication and preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradient-boosted models demonstrated equivalent or better performance compared to traditional mixed effects models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient-boosted models demonstrated equivalent or better performance compared to traditional mixed effects models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparative model performance; framing as a conclusion does not change the claim content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning approach is ideal for analyzing behavioral data in animal models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The broader generalization about ML being 'ideal' for animal behavioral data is not present in the publication claims and appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning-based model developed using the amino acid binary profile of terminal residues achieved maximum AUC of 0.93, 0.98, and 0.94 for gram-positive, gram-negative, and gram-variable bacteria, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed alignment-free methods for predicting antibacterial peptides using machine/deep learning techniques achieved maximum AUC of 0.93, 0.98, and 0.94 for gram-positive, gram-negative, and gram-variable bacteria, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUC results for the predictive methods; differences are wording/terminology rather than content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method performs better than existing methods when compared on an independent dataset. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning-based model developed using the amino acid binary profile of terminal residues outperformed existing methods when evaluated on an independent dataset. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same comparative performance conclusion with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a novel approach that enables the prediction of antibacterial peptides against several bacterial groups, including gram-positive, gram-negative, and gram-variable bacteria. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed alignment-free methods for predicting antibacterial peptides using machine/deep learning techniques achieved maximum AUC of 0.93, 0.98, and 0.94 for gram-positive, gram-negative, and gram-variable bacteria, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the developed alignment-free ML/deep-learning methods and their performance; this clarifies and strengthens the methodological description of the preprint's contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attempts to develop hybrid or ensemble methods by merging machine learning models with similarity and motif-based techniques did not yield any improvements. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not mention this negative result about hybrid/ensemble attempts, so this limitation appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a comprehensive set of methods tailored to different bacterial groups for predicting antibacterial peptides. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed alignment-free methods for predicting antibacterial peptides using machine/deep learning techniques achieved maximum AUC of 0.93, 0.98, and 0.94 for gram-positive, gram-negative, and gram-variable bacteria, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents concrete performance metrics for methods across bacterial groups, clarifying and strengthening the preprint's contribution claim (hence an amplification in confidence/support).

alignments.pub_claim_text: A user-friendly web server, standalone package, and pip package have been developed to facilitate peptide-based therapeutics. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces software/tooling outputs (web server and packages) that were not present among the preprint claims, so this is a new added contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AtacWorks accurately denoises chromatin accessibility at base-pair resolution from low-coverage ATAC-seq data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AtacWorks is a deep learning toolkit that can denoise sequencing coverage and identify regulatory peaks at base-pair resolution from low-quality ATAC-seq data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the denoising and base-pair resolution claim but frames AtacWorks as a toolkit and softens certainty (medium), so the claim is clarified and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AtacWorks can identify peaks from low-coverage bulk sequencing of unseen cell types and experimental conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained by AtacWorks can detect peaks from cell types not seen in the training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the unseen-cell-type peak detection claim but drops explicit mention of bulk samples and experimental-condition generalization, narrowing and clarifying the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AtacWorks can obtain high-quality results from as few as 50 aggregate single-cell ATAC-seq profiles. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AtacWorks enhances the sensitivity of single-cell experiments by producing results on par with those of conventional methods using ~10 times as many cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a comparable sensitivity claim (achieving similar results with roughly an order of magnitude fewer cells) but reframes the numeric example rather than repeating the specific '50' figure, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AtacWorks can be adapted for cross-modality prediction of transcription factor footprints and ChIP-seq peaks from low input ATAC-seq. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or emphasize cross-modality prediction (TF footprints/ChIP) from ATAC-seq, so this preprint claim is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AtacWorks identifies regulatory regions that are differentially-accessible between rare lineage-primed subpopulations of hematopoietic stem cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AtacWorks can enable new biological discoveries by identifying active regulatory regions associated with lineage priming in rare subpopulations of hematopoietic stem cells. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the biological-discovery claim in slightly more general/interpretive language (enabling discoveries), but the substantive finding about regulatory regions in rare HSC subpopulations is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed identification framework can measure minute quantities of microbes using artificial neural networks and three-dimensional quantitative phase imaging. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our microscopy-based framework identifies the pathogen from single to few cells. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the single-to-few-cells identification capability using similar phrasing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework successfully distinguishes between a total of 19 species of bacterial bloodstream infection pathogens. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the identification of 19 bacterial species that cause bloodstream infections, achieving an accuracy of 82.5% from an individual bacterial cell or cluster. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the 19-species identification result but adds an explicit accuracy metric (82.5%), thereby clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the framework is 99.9% when measuring ten bacteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our accuracy increases with multiple measurements, reaching 99.9% with seven different measurements of cells or clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published result still reports 99.9% accuracy but attributes it to seven measurements rather than ten, so the numeric claim has been revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can serve as an effective advisory tool for clinicians during the initial antibiotic prescription. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our framework can serve as a beneficial advisory tool for clinicians during the initial treatment of infections. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the clinical-advisory conclusion with similar wording and confidence, slightly reframing it as 'beneficial' during initial treatment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a species- and dataset-independent transfer learning framework (LAmbDA) to train models on multiple datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LAmbDA is model- and dataset-independent, demonstrating its generalizability to diverse data types. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the framework's model- and dataset-independence and generalizability, consistent with the preprint description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LAmbDA achieved high accuracy in labeling cellular subtypes with weighted accuracy of 91% for pancreas and 78% for brain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LAmbDA framework achieved high accuracy in labeling cellular subtypes with weighted accuracies of 90% for simulated 1 datasets, 94% for simulated 2 datasets, 88% for pancreas datasets, and 66% for brain datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different, dataset-specific accuracy numbers (e.g., 88% pancreas, 66% brain) vs. the preprint's figures, so the quantitative claim has been revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LAmbDA Feedforward 1 Layer Neural Network achieved higher weighted accuracy in labeling cellular subtypes than CaSTLe or MetaNeighbor in brain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LAmbDA outperformed two state-of-the-art methods, scmap and CaSTLe, in labeling cellular subtypes in brain datasets, achieving 66% accuracy compared to 60% and 32%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms outperforming competitors but compares against scmap and CaSTLe (with specific percentages) rather than the exact comparator list in the preprint, so the comparative claim was revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LAmbDA Feedforward 1 Layer Neural Network was the only method to correctly predict ambiguous cellular subtype labels in both pancreas and brain compared to CaSTLe and MetaNeighbor. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LAmbDA framework correctly predicted ambiguous cellular subtype labels across datasets in 88% of test cases, outperforming CaSTLe (63%), scmap (50%), and MetaNeighbor (50%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports quantitative outperformance (percentages) rather than asserting sole success; thus the strong exclusivity claim was revised to quantified superiority.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LAmbDA is model- and dataset-independent and generalizable to diverse data types representing an advance in biocomputing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LAmbDA is model- and dataset-independent, demonstrating its generalizability to diverse data types. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the framework's claimed model- and dataset-independence and generalizability with similar emphasis and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chemical structure can predict drug function as defined by MeSH 'Therapeutic Use' classes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Drug function can be predicted from only a chemical structure. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim but expresses it more strongly (higher certainty), so the claim is effectively amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chemical images with convolutional neural networks and molecular fingerprints with random forests outperform previous predictions based on drug-induced transcriptomic changes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Chemical-structure-derived drug classification methods outperformed previous predictions using drug-induced transcriptomic changes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the comparative performance finding with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed strategy can leverage more training data to significantly improve predictive accuracy to 83-88%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our strategy can leverage more training data to significantly improve predictive accuracy to 83-88%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the exact performance-improvement claim and numeric range, unchanged in meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modeling strategy is effective for multi-label classification, including prediction of side effects and drug repurposing opportunities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modeling strategy is effective for multilabel classification, including prediction of side effects and drug-repurposing opportunities. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms multilabel effectiveness and elevates the claim's certainty (conclusion/high), clarifying and strengthening the preprint's result-oriented phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Liquid neural networks, or 'liquid brains', display cognitive abilities such as learning and decision-making despite lacking fixed, long-term agent-agent connections. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Liquid neural networks, or 'liquid brains', display cognitive abilities such as learning and decision-making despite lacking fixed, long-term agent-agent connections. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion verbatim with the same emphasis and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Liquid brains share key properties with standard neural systems in terms of formal descriptions, but differ significantly in other aspects. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Liquid brains share key properties with standard neural systems in terms of formal descriptions, but differ significantly in other aspects. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This claim is carried forward unchanged from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attractors in liquid brains are based on population abundances rather than connection weights. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attractors in liquid brains are based on population abundances rather than connection weights, indicating a different mechanism of operation compared to standard neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the attractor mechanism claim but frames it with slightly lower certainty and adds interpretive context, clarifying while tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some liquid systems utilize fluctuations in a manner similar to cortical networks, indicating a significant role of criticality in responding to external signals. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some liquid systems utilize fluctuations in a manner similar to cortical networks, suggesting that criticality plays a significant role in their response to external signals. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this result with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contextual Out-of-Distribution Integration (CODI) generates synthetic data that integrates unrepresented sources of variation into a given molecular fingerprint dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CODI generates synthetic data that integrate unrepresented sources of variation into a given molecular fingerprint dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is a near-verbatim restatement of the preprint contribution with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using CODI enables an ML model to better generalize to samples beyond the initial training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By augmenting a dataset with out-of-distribution variance, CODI enables an ML model to better generalize to samples beyond the seed training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same result and intent, rephrasing with equivalent detail about augmenting with OOD variance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating CODI into the classification workflow consistently led to significantly improved classification accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating CODI into the classification workflow consistently leads to increased robustness against data variability and improved predictive accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the accuracy improvement claim but adds explicit emphasis on robustness to variability, broadening the stated benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CODI minimizes the requirement of collecting extensive experimental observations while enhancing the robustness of trained ML models for improved disease detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CODI enhances the robustness of trained ML models for improved disease detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the robustness-for-disease-detection conclusion but omits the preprint's claim about minimizing experimental data collection, narrowing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed nanobody (Nb Ab.2) demonstrates high-affinity binding to the spike protein of SARS-CoV-2 with affinities of 9 nM and 60 nM for the receptor-binding domain protein and virus-like particle, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed nanobody (Nb Ab.2) demonstrates high-affinity binding to the spike protein of SARS-CoV-2 with affinities of 9 nM and 60 nM for the receptor-binding domain protein and virus-like particle, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the quantitative affinity results exactly; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a CDR-grafting approach combined with computer simulation and machine learning for designing nanobodies targeting specific epitopes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CDR grafting approach combined with computer simulation and machine learning can effectively design nanobodies targeting specific epitopes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational pipeline developed in this study can efficiently design high-affinity nanobodies with diagnostic and prophylactic potential against various viral targets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study shows that the computational pipeline can be used to efficiently design high-affinity nanobodies with diagnostic and prophylactic potential. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
